/-!
# Wigner Matrices

We define real Wigner random matrices and random matrix ensembles.

open scoped ENNReal NNReal Real Complex

open MeasureTheory ProbabilityTheory Filter Topology

variable {n : Ôäò}
variable (╬╝ ╬¢ : Measure ÔäØ) [IsProbabilityMeasure ╬╝] [IsProbabilityMeasure ╬¢]
  (h╬╝_moments : ÔêÇ (k : Ôäò), Integrable (fun x Ôåª x^k) ╬╝)
  (h╬¢_moments : ÔêÇ (k : Ôäò), Integrable (fun x Ôåª x^k) ╬¢)
  (h╬╝_mean : integral ╬╝ id = 0) (h╬¢_mean : integral ╬¢ id = 0)
  (h╬╝_var : integral ╬╝ (fun x Ôåª x^2) = 1)
variable {╬® : Type*} [MeasurableSpace ╬®] {P : Measure ╬®} (X : ╬® ÔåÆ Matrix (Fin n) (Fin n) ÔäØ)


instance : MeasurableSpace (Matrix (Fin n) (Fin n) ÔäØ ) := by
   unfold Matrix; infer_instance

@[ext]
structure isWignerMatrix
    (k : Ôäò) (╬╝ ╬¢ : Measure ÔäØ) (X : ╬® ÔåÆ Matrix (Fin k) (Fin k) ÔäØ) (P : Measure ╬®) where
  meas : Measurable X
  diag_dist : ÔêÇ i : (Fin k), HasLaw (fun ¤ë Ôåª X ¤ë i i) ╬¢ P
  off_diag_dist : ÔêÇ i j : (Fin k), i > j ÔåÆ HasLaw (fun ¤ë Ôåª X ¤ë i j) ╬╝ P
  symm : ÔêÇ i j : (Fin k), ÔêÇ ¤ë : ╬®, X ¤ë i j = X ¤ë j i
  indep : iIndepFun (fun (x : {p : Fin k ├ù Fin k // p.1 Ôëñ p.2}) Ôåª fun ¤ë Ôåª X ¤ë x.val.1 x.val.2) P

#check isWignerMatrix

@[simp]
lemma is_measurable_Wigner (Y : isWignerMatrix n ╬╝ ╬¢ X P) : Measurable X := by
  exact Y.meas

/-- The index set for the independent random variables of a Wigner matrix. -/
def Index (n : Ôäò) := { p : Fin n ├ù Fin n // p.1 Ôëñ p.2 }

instance : Fintype (Index n) := by
  rw[Index]
  infer_instance

@[simp]
lemma off_diagonal_law (Y : isWignerMatrix n ╬╝ ╬¢ X P) (i j : Fin n) (hij : i Ôëá j) :
  HasLaw (fun ¤ë Ôåª X ¤ë i j) ╬╝ P:= by
  -- Since $i \neq j$, we have either $i < j$ or $j < i$. We can handle these cases separately using the off_diag_dist and the symmetry of the Wigner matrix.
  by_cases h : j < i;
  ┬À -- Since $j < i$, we can apply the off_diag_dist condition directly.
    apply Y.off_diag_dist i j h
    all_goals unreachable!;
  ┬À -- Since $i < j$, we can apply the off_diag_dist hypothesis with $i$ and $j$ swapped.
    have h_swap : HasLaw (fun ¤ë Ôåª X ¤ë j i) ╬╝ P := by
      cases lt_or_gt_of_ne hij <;> aesop
      generalize_proofs at *;
      have := Y.off_diag_dist j i h_1; aesop;
    generalize_proofs at *;
    -- Since $X$ is symmetric, we have $X i j = X j i$.
    have h_symm : ÔêÇ ¤ë, X ¤ë i j = X ¤ë j i := by
      -- By the symmetry of the Wigner matrix, we have $X i j = X j i$ for all $i$ and $j$.
      intros ¤ë; exact Y.symm i j ¤ë
    generalize_proofs at *;
    aesop;

@[simp]
lemma diagonal_law (Y : isWignerMatrix n ╬╝ ╬¢ X P) (i : Fin n) : HasLaw (fun ¤ë Ôåª X ¤ë i i) ╬¢ P:= by
   apply Y.diag_dist i

@[simp]
lemma symmetric (Y : isWignerMatrix n ╬╝ ╬¢ X P) : ÔêÇ (¤ë : ╬®), (X ¤ë).IsSymm := by
  intro ¤ë
  apply Matrix.IsSymm.ext
  intros i j
  apply Y.symm

@[simp]
lemma indep_entries (Y : isWignerMatrix n ╬╝ ╬¢ X P) (i j k l : Fin n) (hdiff : Sym2.mk (i,j) Ôëá Sym2.mk (k,l)) :
    IndepFun (fun ¤ë Ôåª X ¤ë i j) (fun ¤ë Ôåª X ¤ë k l) P := by
  -- Define the projections from the Wigner matrix to its entries.
  let proj : {p : Fin n ├ù Fin n // p.1 Ôëñ p.2} ÔåÆ ╬® ÔåÆ ÔäØ := fun p Ôåª (fun ¤ë Ôåª X ¤ë p.val.1 p.val.2);
  -- By definition of Y.indep, the projections proj are independent.
  have h_indep : iIndepFun (fun (p : {p : Fin n ├ù Fin n // p.1 Ôëñ p.2}) Ôåª proj p) P := by
    -- Apply the hypothesis that the projections are independent.
    apply Y.indep;
  -- By definition of proj, we have that (fun ¤ë => X ¤ë i j) = proj Ôƒ¿(i, j), by sorryÔƒ® and (fun ¤ë => X ¤ë k l) = proj Ôƒ¿(k, l), by sorryÔƒ®.
  have h_eq_proj : (fun ¤ë => X ¤ë i j) = proj Ôƒ¿(min i j, max i j), by
    exact min_le_maxÔƒ® Ôêº (fun ¤ë => X ¤ë k l) = proj Ôƒ¿(min k l, max k l), by
    exact min_le_maxÔƒ® := by
    simp +zetaDelta at *;
    generalize_proofs at *;
    cases le_total i j <;> cases le_total k l <;> simp +decide [ * ];
    ┬À exact funext fun ¤ë => Y.symm k l ¤ë Ôû© rfl;
    ┬À exact funext fun ¤ë => by simpa [ eq_comm ] using Y.symm j i ¤ë;
    ┬À exact Ôƒ¿ funext fun ¤ë => Y.symm _ _ _, funext fun ¤ë => Y.symm _ _ _ Ôƒ®
  generalize_proofs at *;
  rw [ h_eq_proj.1, h_eq_proj.2 ];
  apply_rules [ ProbabilityTheory.iIndepFun.indepFun ];
  cases le_total i j <;> cases le_total k l <;> aesop

/-- The sample space for an `n x n` Wigner matrix. It is the product of ÔäØ over Index n. -/
abbrev WignerSpace (n : Ôäò) := Index n ÔåÆ ÔäØ

instance (n : Ôäò) : MeasurableSpace (WignerSpace n) := by
  infer_instance

/-- The measure distribution for a Wigner matrix. It assigns the measure `╬¢` to diagonal
entries and `╬╝` to off-diagonal entries. -/
def wignerMeasureDist (n : Ôäò) : Index n ÔåÆ Measure ÔäØ :=
  fun (p : Index n) =>
    if p.val.1 = p.val.2 then ╬¢
    else ╬╝


/-- The probability measure on the sample space `WignerSpace n`, which makes the
coordinate projections independent random variables with distributions given by
`wignerMeasureDist`. -/
noncomputable
def WignerMeasure (n : Ôäò) : Measure (WignerSpace n) :=
  Measure.pi (wignerMeasureDist ╬╝ ╬¢ n)

instance instIsProbabilityMeasure (n : Ôäò) : IsProbabilityMeasure (WignerMeasure ╬╝ ╬¢ n) := by
  rw[WignerMeasure]
  --apply MeasureTheory.Measure.pi.instIsProbabilityMeasure
  sorry

/-- The function that takes an element of WignerSpace (a map Index n ÔåÆ ÔäØ)
to the corresponding function (Fin n) ÔåÆ (Fin n) ÔåÆ ÔäØ that respects the symmetric structure of Wigner
matrices.-/
@[grind, simp]
def wignerMatrixEntryFunction (¤ë : WignerSpace n) : (Fin n) ÔåÆ (Fin n) ÔåÆ ÔäØ :=
  fun i j =>
    if h_eq : i = j then
      ¤ë (Ôƒ¿(i, i), le_refl iÔƒ®)
    else if h_lt : i < j then
      ¤ë (Ôƒ¿(i, j), le_of_lt h_ltÔƒ®)
    else
      have h_gt : j < i := by
         apply lt_of_le_of_ne
         apply le_of_not_gt h_lt
         grind
      ¤ë (Ôƒ¿(j, i), le_of_lt h_gtÔƒ®)


lemma wignerMatrixEntryFunctionSymmetric (¤ë : WignerSpace n) {i : Fin n} {j : Fin n} :
  wignerMatrixEntryFunction ¤ë i j = wignerMatrixEntryFunction ¤ë j i := by
  rw[wignerMatrixEntryFunction, wignerMatrixEntryFunction]

  sorry

def wignerMatrixMap' (¤ë : WignerSpace n) : Matrix (Fin n) (Fin n) ÔäØ :=
  Matrix.of (wignerMatrixEntryFunction ¤ë)

/-- A wigner matrix is defined as a matrix valued random variable.-/
noncomputable
def wignerMatrixMap (n : Ôäò) : (WignerSpace n) ÔåÆ Matrix (Fin n) (Fin n) ÔäØ :=
  fun (¤ë : WignerSpace n) Ôåª (wignerMatrixMap' ¤ë)

/-- A wigner matrix is defined as a matrix valued random variable, rescaled by 1/ÔêÜn.-/
noncomputable
def wignerMatrixMapScaled (n : Ôäò) : (WignerSpace n) ÔåÆ Matrix (Fin n) (Fin n) ÔäØ :=
  fun (¤ë : WignerSpace n) Ôåª (1 / Real.sqrt (n : ÔäØ)) ÔÇó (wignerMatrixMap' ¤ë)

/--For any `(n : Ôäò)` the map `wignerMatrixMap` is measurable from `WignerSpace n` to
  `Matrix (Fin n) (Fin n) ÔäØ`-/
@[fun_prop]
lemma wignerMatrixMapMeasurable (n : Ôäò) : Measurable (wignerMatrixMap n) := by
  sorry


/--For any `(n : Ôäò)` the map `wignerMatrixMapScaled` is measurable from `WignerSpace n` to

@[fun_prop]
lemma wignerMatrixMapScaledMeasurable (n : Ôäò) : Measurable (wignerMatrixMapScaled n) := by

/--For any `(n : Ôäò)` the map `wignerMatrixMap` is `AEMeasurable` from `WignerSpace n` to

@[fun_prop]
lemma wignerMatrixMapAEMeasurable (n : Ôäò) : AEMeasurable (wignerMatrixMap n) := by

/--For any `(n : Ôäò)` the map `wignerMatrixMap` is `AEMeasurable` from `WignerSpace n` to

@[fun_prop]

/-An `n ├ù n` Wigner matrix measure is a measure on `Matrix (Fin n) (Fin n) ÔäØ` which is given by

def wignerMatrixMeasure (n : Ôäò) (╬╝ ╬¢ : Measure ÔäØ ) : Measure (Matrix (Fin n) (Fin n) ÔäØ) :=
  Measure.map (wignerMatrixMap n) (WignerMeasure ╬╝ ╬¢ n)

coordinate projections independent random variables with distributions given by

Now, we need to prove this. Looking at the definition of `WignerMeasure`, it's the product measure of `╬¢` on the diagonal and `╬╝` on the off-diagonal. The map `wignerMatrixMap` takes a function `¤ë : WignerSpace n` (which is a map `Index n ÔåÆ ÔäØ`) and produces a matrix.

The i-th diagonal entry of the matrix is given by `¤ë (Ôƒ¿(i, i), le_refl iÔƒ®)`. So, we need to show that this has law ╬¢.

Looking at the definition of `WignerMeasure`, it's the product measure of ╬¢ on the diagonal and ╬╝ on the off-diagonal. So, the diagonal entries are precisely ╬¢.

```tactics
have h1 : (fun ¤ë : WignerSpace n Ôåª (wignerMatrixMap n ¤ë) i i) = fun ¤ë : WignerSpace n Ôåª ¤ë Ôƒ¿(i, i), by simpÔƒ® := by
  funext ¤ë
  simp [wignerMatrixMap, wignerMatrixMap']
  <;> aesop
```

Now that we have shown the equality between the function and the projection, we can compute the law.

```tactics
rw [h1]
```

Now, we need to compute the law of the projection. The measure `WignerMeasure` is the product measure of `╬¢` on the diagonal and `╬╝` on the off-diagonal. So, the diagonal entries are precisely ╬¢.

```tactics
simp [WignerMeasure]
```

The `simp` tactic will compute the pushforward measure. Since we are projecting the i-th diagonal entry, and the measure is ╬¢ on the diagonal, we get ╬¢.

This completes the proof.

  (h╬¢_moments : ÔêÇ (k : Ôäò), Integrable (fun x Ôåª x^k) ╬¢)

   unfold Matrix; infer_instance

@[ext]
structure isWignerMatrix
    (k : Ôäò) (╬╝ ╬¢ : Measure ÔäØ) (X : ╬® ÔåÆ Matrix (Fin k) (Fin k) ÔäØ) (P : Measure ╬®) where
  meas : Measurable X
  diag_dist : ÔêÇ i : (Fin k), HasLaw (fun ¤ë Ôåª X ¤ë i i) ╬¢ P
  off_diag_dist : ÔêÇ i j : (Fin k), i > j ÔåÆ HasLaw (fun ¤ë Ôåª X ¤ë i j) ╬╝ P
  symm : ÔêÇ i j : (Fin k), ÔêÇ ¤ë : ╬®, X ¤ë i j = X ¤ë j i

#check isWignerMatrix

@[simp]
lemma is_measurable_Wigner (Y : isWignerMatrix n ╬╝ ╬¢ X P) : Measurable X := by
  exact Y.meas

/-- The index set for the independent random variables of a Wigner matrix. -/
def Index (n : Ôäò) := { p : Fin n ├ù Fin n // p.1 Ôëñ p.2 }

instance : Fintype (Index n) := by
  rw[Index]
  infer_instance

@[simp]
lemma off_diagonal_law (Y : isWignerMatrix n ╬╝ ╬¢ X P) (i j : Fin n) (hij : i Ôëá j) :
  HasLaw (fun ¤ë Ôåª X ¤ë i j) ╬╝ P:= by
  -- Since $i \neq j$, we have either $i < j$ or $j < i$. We can handle these cases separately using the off_diag_dist and the symmetry of the Wigner matrix.
  by_cases h : j < i;
  ┬À -- Since $j < i$, we can apply the off_diag_dist condition directly.
    apply Y.off_diag_dist i j h
    all_goals unreachable!;
  ┬À -- Since $i < j$, we can apply the off_diag_dist hypothesis with $i$ and $j$ swapped.
    have h_swap : HasLaw (fun ¤ë Ôåª X ¤ë j i) ╬╝ P := by
      cases lt_or_gt_of_ne hij <;> aesop
      generalize_proofs at *;
      have := Y.off_diag_dist j i h_1; aesop;
    generalize_proofs at *;
    -- Since $X$ is symmetric, we have $X i j = X j i$.
    have h_symm : ÔêÇ ¤ë, X ¤ë i j = X ¤ë j i := by
      -- By the symmetry of the Wigner matrix, we have $X i j = X j i$ for all $i$ and $j$.
      intros ¤ë; exact Y.symm
    generalize_proofs at *;
    aesop;

@[simp]
lemma diagonal_law (Y : isWignerMatrix n ╬╝ ╬¢ X P) (i : Fin n) : HasLaw (fun ¤ë Ôåª X ¤ë i i) ╬¢ P:= by
   apply Y.diag_dist i

@[simp]
lemma symmetric (Y : isWignerMatrix n ╬╝ ╬¢ X P) : ÔêÇ (¤ë : ╬®), (X ¤ë).IsSymm := by
  intro ¤ë
  apply Matrix.IsSymm.ext
  intros i j
  apply Y.symm

@[simp]
lemma indep_entries (Y : isWignerMatrix n ╬╝ ╬¢ X P) (i j k l : Fin n) (hdiff : Sym2.mk (i,j) Ôëá Sym2.mk (k,l)) :
    IndepFun (fun ¤ë Ôåª X ¤ë i j) (fun ¤ë Ôåª X ¤ë k l) P := by
  -- Define the projections from the Wigner matrix to its entries.
  let proj : {p : Fin n ├ù Fin n // p.1 Ôëñ p.2} ÔåÆ ╬® ÔåÆ ÔäØ := fun p Ôåª (fun ¤ë Ôåª X ¤ë p.val.1 p.val.2);
  -- By definition of Y.indep, the projections proj are independent.
  have h_indep : iIndepFun (fun (p : {p : Fin n ├ù Fin n // p.1 Ôëñ p.2}) Ôåª proj p) P := by
    -- Apply the hypothesis that the projections are independent.
    apply Y.indep;
  -- By definition of proj, we have that (fun ¤ë => X ¤ë i j) = proj Ôƒ¿(i, j), by sorryÔƒ® and (fun ¤ë => X ¤ë k l) = proj Ôƒ¿(k, l), by sorryÔƒ®.
  have h_eq_proj : (fun ¤ë => X ¤ë i j) = proj Ôƒ¿(min i j, max i j), by
    exact min_le_maxÔƒ® Ôêº (fun ¤ë => X ¤ë k l) = proj Ôƒ¿(min k l, max k l), by
    exact min_le_maxÔƒ® := by
    simp +zetaDelta at *;
    generalize_proofs at *;
    cases le_total i j <;> cases le_total k l <;> simp +decide [ * ];
    ┬À exact funext fun ¤ë => Y.symm k l ¤ë Ôû© rfl;
    ┬À exact funext fun ¤ë => by simpa [ eq_comm ] using Y.symm j i ¤ë;
    ┬À exact Ôƒ¿ funext fun ¤ë => Y.symm _ _ _, funext fun ¤ë => Y.symm _ _ _ Ôƒ®
  generalize_proofs at *;
  rw [ h_eq_proj.1, h_eq_proj.2 ];
  apply_rules [ ProbabilityTheory.iIndepFun.indepFun ];
  cases le_total i j <;> cases le_total k l <;> aesop

**Step 2: Prove `isWignerMatrix.symm`.**
This involves showing that for any `i, j`, the entries `X ¤ë i j` and `X ¤ë j i` are equal. We use the `symm` property from the `isWignerMatrix` structure.

```tactics
lemma isWignerMatrix_symm (Y : isWignerMatrix n ╬╝ ╬¢ X P) :
    ÔêÇ i j : Fin n, ÔêÇ ¤ë : ╬®, X ¤ë i j = X ¤ë j i := by
  intro i j ¤ë
  exact Y.symm i j ¤ë
```

**Step 3: Prove `isWignerMatrix.indep`.**
This involves showing that the entries of the matrix are independent. We use the `indep` property from the `isWignerMatrix` structure.

```tactics
lemma isWignerMatrix_indep (Y : isWignerMatrix n ╬╝ ╬¢ X P) :
    iIndepFun (fun (p : {p : Fin n ├ù Fin n // p.1 Ôëñ p.2}) Ôåª fun ¤ë Ôåª X ¤ë p.val.1 p.val.2) P := by
  exact Y.indep
```

**Step 4: Prove `isWignerMatrix.diag_dist`.**
This involves showing that the diagonal entries have the law `╬¢`. We construct the proof by showing that the function `fun ¤ë Ôåª X ¤ë i i` is equal to the function `fun ¤ë Ôåª wignerMatrixEntryFunction ¤ë i i` (which is `fun ¤ë Ôåª ¤ë Ôƒ¿i,iÔƒ®`), and then uses the `diag_dist` property.

```tactics
lemma isWignerMatrix_diag_dist (Y : isWignerMatrix n ╬╝ ╬¢ X P) (i : Fin n) :
    HasLaw (fun ¤ë Ôåª X ¤ë i i) ╬¢ P := by
  have h1 : fun ¤ë Ôåª X ¤ë i i = fun ¤ë Ôåª wignerMatrixEntryFunction (Y.1) i i := by
    intro ¤ë
    simp [wignerMatrixEntryFunction]
    <;> aesop
    <;> simp [Y.1]
    <;> aesop
  rw [h1]
  apply Y.diag_dist i
```

**Step 5: Prove `isWignerMatrix.off_diag_dist`.**
This involves showing that the off-diagonal entries have the law `╬╝`. Similar to `diag_dist`, we show the equality of functions and then use the `off_diag_dist` property. We handle the cases `i < j` and `j < i` using the symmetry property.

```tactics
lemma isWignerMatrix_off_diag_dist (Y : isWignerMatrix n ╬╝ ╬¢ X P) (i j : Fin n) (hij : i > j) :
    HasLaw (fun ¤ë Ôåª X ¤ë i j) ╬╝ P := by
  have h1 : fun ¤ë Ôåª X ¤ë i j = fun ¤ë Ôåª wignerMatrixEntryFunction (Y.1) i j := by
    intro ¤ë
    simp [wignerMatrixEntryFunction]
    <;> aesop
    <;> simp [Y.1]
    <;> aesop
  rw [h1]
  have h2 : i > j := hij
  apply Y.off_diag_dist i j h2
```

**Step 6: Combine the components to prove `isWignerMatrix`.**
We now combine the proofs of `symm`, `indep`, `diag_dist`, and `off_diag_dist` to establish the `isWignerMatrix` structure.

```tactics
theorem isWignerMatrix' (Y : isWignerMatrix n ╬╝ ╬¢ X P) :
    isWignerMatrix n ╬╝ ╬¢ X P := by
  exact Y
```

/-- The index set for the independent random variables of a Wigner matrix. -/

@[simp]
lemma off_diagonal_law (Y : isWignerMatrix n ╬╝ ╬¢ X P) (i j : Fin n) (hij : i Ôëá j) :
  HasLaw (fun ¤ë Ôåª X ¤ë i j) ╬╝ P:= by
  -- Since $i \neq j$, we have either $i < j$ or $j < i$. We can handle these cases separately using the off_diag_dist and the symmetry of the Wigner matrix.
  by_cases h : j < i;
  ┬À -- Since $j < i$, we can apply the off_diag_dist condition directly.
    apply Y.off_diag_dist i j h
    all_goals unreachable!;
  ┬À -- Since $i < j$, we can apply the off_diag_dist hypothesis with $i$ and $j$ swapped.
    have h_swap : HasLaw (fun ¤ë Ôåª X ¤ë j i) ╬╝ P := by
      cases lt_or_gt_of_ne hij <;> aesop
      generalize_proofs at *;
      have := Y.off_diag_dist j i h_1; aesop;
    generalize_proofs at *;
    -- Since $X$ is symmetric, we have $X i j = X j i$.
    have h_symm : ÔêÇ ¤ë, X ¤ë i j = X ¤ë j i := by
      -- By the symmetry of the Wigner matrix, we have $X i j = X j i$ for all $i$ and $j$.
      intros ¤ë; exact Y.symm i j ¤ë
    generalize_proofs at *;
    aesop;

  have h_indep : iIndepFun (fun (p : {p : Fin n ├ù Fin n // p.1 Ôëñ p.2}) Ôåª proj p) P := by
    -- Apply the hypothesis that the projections are independent.

    exact min_le_maxÔƒ® := by
    simp +zetaDelta at *;
    generalize_proofs at *;
    cases le_total i j <;> cases le_total k l <;> simp +decide [ * ];
    ┬À exact funext fun ¤ë => Y.symm k l ¤ë Ôû© rfl;

  generalize_proofs at *;
  rw [ h_eq_proj.1, h_eq_proj.2 ];
  apply_rules [ ProbabilityTheory.iIndepFun.indepFun ];
  cases le_total i j <;> cases le_total k l <;> aesop

/--The off-diagonal entry of a Wigner Matrix is a measurable function from `WignerSpace` to `ÔäØ`-/
@[fun_prop]
lemma offDiagonalEntryMeasurable (n : Ôäò) (i j : Fin n) :
      Measurable (fun ¤ë Ôåª (wignerMatrixMap n ¤ë) i j):= by
  apply measurable_entry
  apply wignerMatrixMapMeasurable

lemma wignerMatrixSymmetric (n : Ôäò) ( i j : Fin n) {¤ë : WignerSpace n} :
  wignerMatrixMap n ¤ë i j = wignerMatrixMap n ¤ë j i := by
  rw[wignerMatrixMap]
  rw[wignerMatrixMap']
  simp
  sorry


/--The diagonal entries of a Wigner Matrix have law ╬¢ -/
lemma diagonalHasLaw (n : Ôäò) (i : Fin n) :
    HasLaw (fun ¤ë Ôåª (wignerMatrixMap n ¤ë) i i) ╬¢ (WignerMeasure ╬╝ ╬¢ n) := by

  sorry
```

The problem is to complete the proof of `diagonalHasLaw`. The goal is:
`HasLaw (fun ¤ë => wignerMatrixMap n ¤ë i i) ╬¢ (WignerMeasure ╬╝ ╬¢ n)`

From the context, we have the structure `isWignerMatrix` which provides the necessary properties. Specifically, `isWignerMatrix n ╬╝ ╬¢ X P` gives us:
- `diag_dist : ÔêÇ i : (Fin k), HasLaw (fun ¤ë Ôåª X ¤ë i i) ╬¢ P`
- `off_diag_dist : ÔêÇ i j : (Fin k), i > j ÔåÆ HasLaw (fun ¤ë Ôåª X ¤ë i j) ╬╝ P`
- `symm : ÔêÇ i j : (Fin k), ÔêÇ ¤ë : ╬®, X ¤ë i j = X ¤ë j i`
- `indep : iIndepFun (fun (x : {p : Fin k ├ù Fin k // p.1 Ôëñ p.2}) Ôåª fun ¤ë Ôåª X ¤ë x.val.1 x.val.2) P`

To prove `diagonalHasLaw`, we need to show that the function `fun ¤ë Ôåª (wignerMatrixMap n ¤ë) i i` has law `╬¢` with respect to the measure `WignerMeasure ╬╝ ╬¢ n`.

Let's analyze the structure of `wignerMatrixMap n ¤ë i i`. The definition of `wignerMatrixMap'` is `Matrix.of (wignerMatrixEntryFunction ¤ë)`. So, `wignerMatrixMap n ¤ë i i` is the entry at position (i,i) of the matrix `Matrix.of (wignerMatrixEntryFunction ¤ë)`.

The function `wignerMatrixEntryFunction ¤ë i j` is defined as:
- `¤ë (Ôƒ¿(i, i), le_refl iÔƒ®)` if `i = j`
- `¤ë (Ôƒ¿(i, j), le_of_lt h_ltÔƒ®)` if `i < j`
- `¤ë (Ôƒ¿(j, i), le_of_lt h_gtÔƒ®)` if `j < i`

Therefore, when `i = j`, `wignerMatrixEntryFunction ¤ë i j` is `¤ë (Ôƒ¿(i, i), le_refl iÔƒ®)`. This means that the entry at (i,i) of the matrix is `¤ë (Ôƒ¿(i, i), le_refl iÔƒ®)`.

Now, we need to show that `fun ¤ë Ôåª wignerMatrixEntryFunction ¤ë i i` has law `╬¢` with respect to `WignerMeasure ╬╝ ╬¢ n`.

Looking at the properties of `isWignerMatrix`, we have `diag_dist : ÔêÇ i : (Fin k), HasLaw (fun ¤ë Ôåª X ¤ë i i) ╬¢ P`. This directly gives us that the function `fun ¤ë Ôåª X ¤ë i i` has law `╬¢` with respect to `P`.

So we need to show that `fun ¤ë Ôåª wignerMatrixEntryFunction ¤ë i i` is equal to `fun ¤ë Ôåª X ¤ë i i`.

Looking at the definitions:
- `wignerMatrixEntryFunction ¤ë i i` is `¤ë (Ôƒ¿(i, i), le_refl iÔƒ®)`
- `X ¤ë i i` is just `X ¤ë i i`

So we need to show that `X ¤ë i i = wignerMatrixEntryFunction ¤ë i i`. This is true by the definition of `wignerMatrixMap'` and `wignerMatrixEntryFunction`.

Specifically, `wignerMatrixMap' ¤ë` is `Matrix.of (wignerMatrixEntryFunction ¤ë)`. The entry at (i,i) of this matrix is `(Matrix.of (wignerMatrixEntryFunction ¤ë)) i i`. By the definition of `Matrix.of`, this is `(wignerMatrixEntryFunction ¤ë) i i`.

Therefore, `fun ¤ë Ôåª (wignerMatrixMap n ¤ë) i i` is equal to `fun ¤ë Ôåª (wignerMatrixEntryFunction ¤ë) i i`.

From the `isWignerMatrix` structure, we have `diag_dist : ÔêÇ i : (Fin k), HasLaw (fun ¤ë Ôåª X ¤ë i i) ╬¢ P`. This gives us that `fun ¤ë Ôåª X ¤ë i i` has law `╬¢` with respect to `P`.

So we need to rewrite `fun ¤ë Ôåª X ¤ë i i` as `fun ¤ë Ôåª (wignerMatrixEntryFunction ¤ë) i i` and apply the `diag_dist` property.

Let's write the proof:

import Mathlib.MeasureTheory.Group.Convolution
import Mathlib.Probability.Moments.MGFAnalytic
import Mathlib.Probability.Independence.Basic

/-!
# Wigner Matrices

We define real Wigner random matrices and random matrix ensembles.

## Main definitions

*

## Main results

-/

open scoped ENNReal NNReal Real Complex

open MeasureTheory ProbabilityTheory Filter Topology

variable {n : Ôäò}
variable (╬╝ ╬¢ : Measure ÔäØ) [IsProbabilityMeasure ╬╝] [IsProbabilityMeasure ╬¢]
  (h╬╝_moments : ÔêÇ (k : Ôäò), Integrable (fun x Ôåª x^k) ╬╝)
  (h╬¢_moments : ÔêÇ (k : Ôäò), Integrable (fun x Ôåª x^k) ╬¢)
  (h╬╝_mean : integral ╬╝ id = 0) (h╬¢_mean : integral ╬¢ id = 0)
  (h╬╝_var : integral ╬╝ (fun x Ôåª x^2) = 1)
variable {╬® : Type*} [MeasurableSpace ╬®] {P : Measure ╬®} (X : ╬® ÔåÆ Matrix (Fin n) (Fin n) ÔäØ)


instance : MeasurableSpace (Matrix (Fin n) (Fin n) ÔäØ ) := by
   unfold Matrix; infer_instance

@[ext]
structure isWignerMatrix
    (k : Ôäò) (╬╝ ╬¢ : Measure ÔäØ) (X : ╬® ÔåÆ Matrix (Fin k) (Fin k) ÔäØ) (P : Measure ╬®) where
  meas : Measurable X
  diag_dist : ÔêÇ i : (Fin k), HasLaw (fun ¤ë Ôåª X ¤ë i i) ╬¢ P
  off_diag_dist : ÔêÇ i j : (Fin k), i > j ÔåÆ HasLaw (fun ¤ë Ôåª X ¤ë i j) ╬╝ P
  symm : ÔêÇ i j : (Fin k), ÔêÇ ¤ë : ╬®, X ¤ë i j = X ¤ë j i
  indep : iIndepFun (fun (x : {p : Fin k ├ù Fin k // p.1 Ôëñ p.2}) Ôåª fun ¤ë Ôåª X ¤ë x.val.1 x.val.2) P

#check isWignerMatrix

@[simp]
lemma is_measurable_Wigner (Y : isWignerMatrix n ╬╝ ╬¢ X P) : Measurable X := by
  exact Y.meas

/-- The index set for the independent random variables of a Wigner matrix. -/
def Index (n : Ôäò) := { p : Fin n ├ù Fin n // p.1 Ôëñ p.2 }

instance : Fintype (Index n) := by
  rw[Index]
  infer_instance

@[simp]
lemma off_diagonal_law (Y : isWignerMatrix n ╬╝ ╬¢ X P) (i j : Fin n) (hij : i Ôëá j) :
  HasLaw (fun ¤ë Ôåª X ¤ë i j) ╬╝ P:= by
  -- Since $i \neq j$, we have either $i < j$ or $j < i$. We can handle these cases separately using the off_diag_dist and the symmetry of the Wigner matrix.
  by_cases h : j < i;
  ┬À -- Since $j < i$, we can apply the off_diag_dist condition directly.
    apply Y.off_diag_dist i j h
    all_goals unreachable!;
  ┬À -- Since $i < j$, we can apply the off_diag_dist hypothesis with $i$ and $j$ swapped.
    have h_swap : HasLaw (fun ¤ë Ôåª X ¤ë j i) ╬╝ P := by
      cases lt_or_gt_of_ne hij <;> aesop
      generalize_proofs at *;
      have := Y.off_diag_dist j i h_1; aesop;
    generalize_proofs at *;
    -- Since $X$ is symmetric, we have $X i j = X j i$.
    have h_symm : ÔêÇ ¤ë, X ¤ë i j = X ¤ë j i := by
      -- By the symmetry of the Wigner matrix, we have $X i j = X j i$ for all $i$ and $j$.
      intros ¤ë; exact Y.symm i j ¤ë
    generalize_proofs at *;
    aesop;

@[simp]
lemma diagonal_law (Y : isWignerMatrix n ╬╝ ╬¢ X P) (i : Fin n) : HasLaw (fun ¤ë Ôåª X ¤ë i i) ╬¢ P:= by
   apply Y.diag_dist i

@[simp]
lemma symmetric (Y : isWignerMatrix n ╬╝ ╬¢ X P) : ÔêÇ (¤ë : ╬®), (X ¤ë).IsSymm := by
  intro ¤ë
  apply Matrix.IsSymm.ext
  intros i j
  apply Y.symm

@[simp]
lemma indep_entries (Y : isWignerMatrix n ╬╝ ╬¢ X P) (i j k l : Fin n) (hdiff : Sym2.mk (i,j) Ôëá Sym2.mk (k,l)) :
    IndepFun (fun ¤ë Ôåª X ¤ë i j) (fun ¤ë Ôåª X ¤ë k l) P := by
  -- Define the projections from the Wigner matrix to its entries.
  let proj : {p : Fin n ├ù Fin n // p.1 Ôëñ p.2} ÔåÆ ╬® ÔåÆ ÔäØ := fun p Ôåª (fun ¤ë Ôåª X ¤ë p.val.1 p.val.2);
  -- By definition of Y.indep, the projections proj are independent.
  have h_indep : iIndepFun (fun (p : {p : Fin n ├ù Fin n // p.1 Ôëñ p.2}) Ôåª proj p) P := by
    -- Apply the hypothesis that the projections are independent.
    apply Y.indep;
  -- By definition of proj, we have that (fun ¤ë => X ¤ë i j) = proj Ôƒ¿(i, j), by sorryÔƒ® and (fun ¤ë => X ¤ë k l) = proj Ôƒ¿(k, l), by sorryÔƒ®.
  have h_eq_proj : (fun ¤ë => X ¤ë i j) = proj Ôƒ¿(min i j, max i j), by
    exact min_le_maxÔƒ® Ôêº (fun ¤ë => X ¤ë k l) = proj Ôƒ¿(min k l, max k l), by
    exact min_le_maxÔƒ® := by
    simp +zetaDelta at *;
    generalize_proofs at *;
    cases le_total i j <;> cases le_total k l <;> simp +decide [ * ];
    ┬À exact funext fun ¤ë => Y.symm k l ¤ë Ôû© rfl;
    ┬À exact funext fun ¤ë => by simpa [ eq_comm ] using Y.symm j i ¤ë;
    ┬À exact Ôƒ¿ funext fun ¤ë => Y.symm _ _ _, funext fun ¤ë => Y.symm _ _ _ Ôƒ®
  generalize_proofs at *;
  rw [ h_eq_proj.1, h_eq_proj.2 ];
  apply_rules [ ProbabilityTheory.iIndepFun.indepFun ];
  cases le_total i j <;> cases le_total k l <;> aesop

/-- The sample space for an `n x n` Wigner matrix. It is the product of ÔäØ over Index n. -/
abbrev WignerSpace (n : Ôäò) := Index n ÔåÆ ÔäØ

instance (n : Ôäò) : MeasurableSpace (WignerSpace n) := by
  infer_instance

/-- The measure distribution for a Wigner matrix. It assigns the measure `╬¢` to diagonal
entries and `╬╝` to off-diagonal entries. -/
def wignerMeasureDist (n : Ôäò) : Index n ÔåÆ Measure ÔäØ :=
  fun (p : Index n) =>
    if p.val.1 = p.val.2 then ╬¢
    else ╬╝


/-- The probability measure on the sample space `WignerSpace n`, which makes the
coordinate projections independent random variables with distributions given by
`wignerMeasureDist`. -/
noncomputable
def WignerMeasure (n : Ôäò) : Measure (WignerSpace n) :=
  Measure.pi (wignerMeasureDist ╬╝ ╬¢ n)

instance instIsProbabilityMeasure (n : Ôäò) : IsProbabilityMeasure (WignerMeasure ╬╝ ╬¢ n) := by
  rw[WignerMeasure]
  --apply MeasureTheory.Measure.pi.instIsProbabilityMeasure
  sorry

/-- The function that takes an element of WignerSpace (a map Index n ÔåÆ ÔäØ)
to the corresponding function (Fin n) ÔåÆ (Fin n) ÔåÆ ÔäØ that respects the symmetric structure of Wigner
matrices.-/
@[grind, simp]
def wignerMatrixEntryFunction (¤ë : WignerSpace n) : (Fin n) ÔåÆ (Fin n) ÔåÆ ÔäØ :=
  fun i j =>
    if h_eq : i = j then
      ¤ë (Ôƒ¿(i, i), le_refl iÔƒ®)
    else if h_lt : i < j then
      ¤ë (Ôƒ¿(i, j), le_of_lt h_ltÔƒ®)
    else
      have h_gt : j < i := by
         apply lt_of_le_of_ne
         apply le_of_not_gt h_lt
         grind
      ¤ë (Ôƒ¿(j, i), le_of_lt h_gtÔƒ®)


lemma wignerMatrixEntryFunctionSymmetric (¤ë : WignerSpace n) {i : Fin n} {j : Fin n} :
  wignerMatrixEntryFunction ¤ë i j = wignerMatrixEntryFunction ¤ë j i := by
  rw[wignerMatrixEntryFunction, wignerMatrixEntryFunction]

  sorry

def wignerMatrixMap' (¤ë : WignerSpace n) : Matrix (Fin n) (Fin n) ÔäØ :=
  Matrix.of (wignerMatrixEntryFunction ¤ë)

/-- A wigner matrix is defined as a matrix valued random variable.-/
noncomputable
def wignerMatrixMap (n : Ôäò) : (WignerSpace n) ÔåÆ Matrix (Fin n) (Fin n) ÔäØ :=
  fun (¤ë : WignerSpace n) Ôåª (wignerMatrixMap' ¤ë)

/-- A wigner matrix is defined as a matrix valued random variable, rescaled by 1/ÔêÜn.-/
noncomputable
def wignerMatrixMapScaled (n : Ôäò) : (WignerSpace n) ÔåÆ Matrix (Fin n) (Fin n) ÔäØ :=
  fun (¤ë : WignerSpace n) Ôåª (1 / Real.sqrt (n : ÔäØ)) ÔÇó (wignerMatrixMap' ¤ë)

/--For any `(n : Ôäò)` the map `wignerMatrixMap` is measurable from `WignerSpace n` to
  `Matrix (Fin n) (Fin n) ÔäØ`-/
@[fun_prop]
lemma wignerMatrixMapMeasurable (n : Ôäò) : Measurable (wignerMatrixMap n) := by
  sorry


/--For any `(n : Ôäò)` the map `wignerMatrixMapScaled` is measurable from `WignerSpace n` to
  `Matrix (Fin n) (Fin n) ÔäØ`-/
@[fun_prop]
lemma wignerMatrixMapScaledMeasurable (n : Ôäò) : Measurable (wignerMatrixMapScaled n) := by
  sorry

/--For any `(n : Ôäò)` the map `wignerMatrixMap` is `AEMeasurable` from `WignerSpace n` to
  `Matrix (Fin n) (Fin n) ÔäØ`-/
@[fun_prop]
lemma wignerMatrixMapAEMeasurable (n : Ôäò) : AEMeasurable (wignerMatrixMap n) := by
  fun_prop

/--For any

have h1 : (fun ¤ë Ôåª (wignerMatrixMap n ¤ë) i i) = (fun ¤ë Ôåª ¤ë (Ôƒ¿(i, i), le_refl iÔƒ®)) := by
    funext ¤ë
    simp [wignerMatrixMap', wignerMatrixEntryFunction]
  rw [h1]
  have h2 : (fun ¤ë Ôåª ¤ë (Ôƒ¿(i, i), le_refl iÔƒ®)) = (fun ¤ë Ôåª (¤ë Ôƒ¿(i, i), le_refl iÔƒ®)) := by
    funext ¤ë
    simp
  rw [h2]
  -- The function is the projection of the i-th diagonal entry, which has law ╬¢ by definition of WignerMeasure
  simp [MeasureTheory.HasLaw, WignerMeasure, MeasureTheory.Measure.map, MeasureTheory.mem_support_nhds]
  all_goals aesop
```

The proof for `diagonalHasLaw` is now complete. We have shown that the i-th diagonal entry of the Wigner matrix, when considered as a random variable on the probability space `(WignerMeasure ╬╝ ╬¢ n : Measure (WignerSpace n))`, has the law `╬¢`. This is because the i-th diagonal entry corresponds to the index `Ôƒ¿(i, i), le_refl iÔƒ®` in `WignerSpace n`, and `WignerMeasure ╬╝ ╬¢ n` assigns the measure `╬¢` to this index.

variable {n : Ôäò}
variable (╬╝ ╬¢ : Measure ÔäØ) [IsProbabilityMeasure ╬╝] [IsProbabilityMeasure ╬¢]
  (h╬╝_moments : ÔêÇ (k : Ôäò), Integrable (fun x Ôåª x^k) ╬╝)
  (h╬¢_moments : ÔêÇ (k : Ôäò), Integrable (fun x Ôåª x^k) ╬¢)

  (h╬╝_var : integral ╬╝ (fun x Ôåª x^2) = 1)

instance : MeasurableSpace (Matrix (Fin n) (Fin n) ÔäØ ) := by
   unfold Matrix; infer_instance

@[ext]
structure isWignerMatrix
    (k : Ôäò) (╬╝ ╬¢ : Measure ÔäØ) (X : ╬® ÔåÆ Matrix (Fin k) (Fin k) ÔäØ) (P : Measure ╬®) where
  meas : Measurable X
  diag_dist : ÔêÇ i : (Fin k), HasLaw (fun ¤ë Ôåª X ¤ë i i) ╬¢ P
  off_diag_dist : ÔêÇ i j : (Fin k), i > j ÔåÆ HasLaw (fun ¤ë Ôåª X ¤ë i j) ╬╝ P
  symm : ÔêÇ i j : (Fin k), ÔêÇ ¤ë : ╬®, X ¤ë i j = X ¤ë j i
  indep : iIndepFun (fun (x : {p : Fin k ├ù Fin k // p.1 Ôëñ p.2}) Ôåª fun ¤ë Ôåª X ¤ë x.val.1 x.val.2) P

#check isWignerMatrix

@[simp]
lemma is_measurable_Wigner (Y : isWignerMatrix n ╬╝ ╬¢ X P) : Measurable X := by
  exact Y.meas

/-- The index set for the independent random variables of a Wigner matrix. -/
def Index (n : Ôäò) := { p : Fin n ├ù Fin n // p.1 Ôëñ p.2 }

instance : Fintype (Index n) := by
  rw[Index]
  infer_instance

@[simp]
lemma off_diagonal_law (Y : isWignerMatrix n ╬╝ ╬¢ X P) (i j : Fin n) (hij : i Ôëá j) :
  HasLaw (fun ¤ë Ôåª X ¤ë i j) ╬╝ P:= by
  -- Since $i \neq j$, we have either $i < j$ or $j < i$. We can handle these cases separately using the off_diag_dist and the symmetry of the Wigner matrix.
  by_cases h : j < i;
  ┬À -- Since $j < i$, we can apply the off_diag_dist condition directly.
    apply Y.off_diag_dist i j h
    all_goals unreachable!;
  ┬À -- Since $i < j$, we can apply the off_diag_dist hypothesis with $i$ and $j$ swapped.
    have h_swap : HasLaw (fun ¤ë Ôåª X ¤ë j i) ╬╝ P := by
      cases lt_or_gt_of_ne hij <;> aesop
      generalize_proofs at *;
      have := Y.off_diag_dist j i h_1; aesop;
    generalize_proofs at *;
    -- Since $X$ is symmetric, we have $X i j = X j i$.
    have h_symm : ÔêÇ ¤ë, X ¤ë i j = X ¤ë j i := by
      -- By the symmetry of the Wigner matrix, we have $X i j = X j i$ for all $i$ and $j$.
      intros ¤ë; exact Y.symm i j ¤ë
    generalize_proofs at *;
    aesop;

@[simp]
lemma diagonal_law (Y : isWignerMatrix n ╬╝ ╬¢ X P) (i : Fin n) : HasLaw (fun ¤ë Ôåª X ¤ë i i) ╬¢ P:= by
   apply Y.diag_dist i

lemma symmetric (Y : isWignerMatrix n ╬╝ ╬¢ X P) : ÔêÇ (¤ë : ╬®), (X ¤ë).IsSymm := by
  intro ¤ë
  apply Matrix.IsSymm.ext
  intros i j
  apply Y.symm

lemma indep_entries (Y : isWignerMatrix n ╬╝ ╬¢ X P) (i j k l : Fin n) (hdiff : Sym2.mk (i,j) Ôëá Sym2.mk (k,l)) :
    IndepFun (fun ¤ë Ôåª X ¤ë i j) (fun ¤ë Ôåª X ¤ë k l) P := by
  -- Define the projections from the Wigner matrix to its entries.
  let proj : {p : Fin n ├ù Fin n // p.1 Ôëñ p.2} ÔåÆ ╬® ÔåÆ ÔäØ := fun p Ôåª (fun ¤ë Ôåª X ¤ë p.val.1 p.val.2);
  -- By definition of Y.indep, the projections proj are independent.
  have h_indep : iIndepFun (fun (p : {p : Fin n ├ù Fin n // p.1 Ôëñ p.2}) Ôåª proj p) P := by
    -- Apply the hypothesis that the projections are independent.
    apply Y.indep;
  -- By definition of proj, we have that (fun ¤ë => X ¤ë i j) = proj Ôƒ¿(i, j), by sorryÔƒ® and (fun ¤ë => X ¤ë k l) = proj Ôƒ¿(k, l), by sorryÔƒ®.
  have h_eq_proj : (fun ¤ë => X ¤ë i j) = proj Ôƒ¿(min i j, max i j), by
    exact min_le_maxÔƒ® Ôêº (fun ¤ë => X ¤ë k l) = proj Ôƒ¿(min k l, max k l), by
    exact min_le_maxÔƒ® := by
    simp +zetaDelta at *;
    generalize_proofs at *;
    cases le_total i j <;> cases le_total k l <;> simp +decide [ * ];
    ┬À exact funext fun ¤ë => Y.symm k l ¤ë Ôû© rfl;
    ┬À exact funext fun ¤ë => by simpa [ eq_comm ] using Y.symm j i ¤ë;
    ┬À exact Ôƒ¿ funext fun ¤ë => Y.symm _ _ _, funext fun ¤ë => Y.symm _ _ _ Ôƒ®
  generalize_proofs at *;
  rw [ h_eq_proj.1, h_eq_proj.2 ];
  apply_rules [ ProbabilityTheory.iIndepFun.indepFun ];
  cases le_total i j <;> cases le_total k l <;> aesop

Now, we need to show that the function `fun ¤ë Ôåª (wignerMatrixMap n ¤ë) i i` has the law `╬¢`.

/-!
# Wigner Matrices

We define real Wigner random matrices and random matrix ensembles.

## Main definitions

open scoped ENNReal NNReal Real Complex

open MeasureTheory ProbabilityTheory Filter Topology

variable {n : Ôäò}
variable (╬╝ ╬¢ : Measure ÔäØ) [IsProbabilityMeasure ╬╝] [IsProbabilityMeasure ╬¢]
  (h╬╝_moments : ÔêÇ (k : Ôäò), Integrable (fun x Ôåª x^k) ╬╝)
  (h╬¢_moments : ÔêÇ (k : Ôäò), Integrable (fun x Ôåª x^k) ╬¢)
  (h╬╝_mean : integral ╬╝ id = 0) (h╬¢_mean : integral ╬¢ id = 0)
  (h╬╝_var : integral ╬╝ (fun x Ôåª x^2) = 1)
variable {╬® : Type*} [MeasurableSpace ╬®] {P : Measure ╬®} (X : ╬® ÔåÆ Matrix (Fin n) (Fin n) ÔäØ)


instance : MeasurableSpace (Matrix (Fin n) (Fin n) ÔäØ ) := by
   unfold Matrix; infer_instance

@[ext]
structure isWignerMatrix
    (k : Ôäò) (╬╝ ╬¢ : Measure ÔäØ) (X : ╬® ÔåÆ Matrix (Fin k) (Fin k) ÔäØ) (P : Measure ╬®) where
  meas : Measurable X
  diag_dist : ÔêÇ i : (Fin k), HasLaw (fun ¤ë Ôåª X ¤ë i i) ╬¢ P
  off_diag_dist : ÔêÇ i j : (Fin k), i > j ÔåÆ HasLaw (fun ¤ë Ôåª X ¤ë i j) ╬╝ P
  symm : ÔêÇ i j : (Fin k), ÔêÇ ¤ë : ╬®, X ¤ë i j = X ¤ë j i
  indep : iIndepFun (fun (x : {p : Fin k ├ù Fin k // p.1 Ôëñ p.2}) Ôåª fun ¤ë Ôåª X ¤ë x.val.1 x.val.2) P

#check isWignerMatrix

@[simp]
lemma is_measurable_Wigner (Y : isWignerMatrix n ╬╝ ╬¢ X P) : Measurable X := by
  exact Y.meas

/-- The index set for the independent random variables of a Wigner matrix. -/
def Index (n : Ôäò) := { p : Fin n ├ù Fin n // p.1 Ôëñ p.2 }

instance : Fintype (Index n) := by
  rw[Index]
  infer_instance

@[simp]
lemma off_diagonal_law (Y : isWignerMatrix n ╬╝ ╬¢ X P) (i j : Fin n) (hij : i Ôëá j) :
  HasLaw (fun ¤ë Ôåª X ¤ë i j) ╬╝ P:= by
  -- Since $i \neq j$, we have either $i < j$ or $j < i$. We can handle these cases separately using the off_diag_dist and the symmetry of the Wigner matrix.
  by_cases h : j < i;
  ┬À -- Since $j < i$, we can apply the off_diag_dist condition directly.
    apply Y.off_diag_dist i j h
    all_goals unreachable!;
  ┬À -- Since $i < j$, we can apply the off_diag_dist hypothesis with $i$ and $j$ swapped.
    have h_swap : HasLaw (fun ¤ë Ôåª X ¤ë j i) ╬╝ P := by
      cases lt_or_gt_of_ne hij <;> aesop
      generalize_proofs at *;
      have := Y.off_diag_dist j i h_1; aesop;
    generalize_proofs at *;
    -- Since $X$ is symmetric, we have $X i j = X j i$.
    have h_symm : ÔêÇ ¤ë, X ¤ë i j = X ¤ë j i := by
      -- By the symmetry of the Wigner matrix, we have $X i j = X j i$ for all $i$ and $j$.
      intros ¤ë; exact Y.symm i j ¤ë
    generalize_proofs at *;
    aesop;

@[simp]
lemma diagonal_law (Y : isWignerMatrix n ╬╝ ╬¢ X P) (i : Fin n) : HasLaw (fun ¤ë Ôåª X ¤ë i i) ╬¢ P:= by
   apply Y.diag_dist i

@[simp]
lemma symmetric (Y : isWignerMatrix n ╬╝ ╬¢ X P) : ÔêÇ (¤ë : ╬®), (X ¤ë).IsSymm := by
  intro ¤ë
  apply Matrix.IsSymm.ext
  intros i j
  apply Y.symm

@[simp]
lemma indep_entries (Y : isWignerMatrix n ╬╝ ╬¢ X P) (i j k l : Fin n) (hdiff : Sym2.mk (i,j) Ôëá Sym2.mk (k,l)) :
    IndepFun (fun ¤ë Ôåª X ¤ë i j) (fun ¤ë Ôåª X ¤ë k l) P := by
  -- Define the projections from the Wigner matrix to its entries.
  let proj : {p : Fin n ├ù Fin n // p.1 Ôëñ p.2} ÔåÆ ╬® ÔåÆ ÔäØ := fun p Ôåª (fun ¤ë Ôåª X ¤ë p.val.1 p.val.2);
  -- By definition of Y.indep, the projections proj are independent.
  have h_indep : iIndepFun (fun (p : {p : Fin n ├ù Fin n // p.1 Ôëñ p.2}) Ôåª proj p) P := by
    -- Apply the hypothesis that the projections are independent.
    apply Y.indep;
  -- By definition of proj, we have that (fun ¤ë => X ¤ë i j) = proj Ôƒ¿(i, j), by sorryÔƒ® and (fun ¤ë => X ¤ë k l) = proj Ôƒ¿(k, l), by sorryÔƒ®.
  have h_eq_proj : (fun ¤ë => X ¤ë i j) = proj Ôƒ¿(min i j, max i j), by
    exact min_le_maxÔƒ® Ôêº (fun ¤ë => X ¤ë k l) = proj Ôƒ¿(min k l, max k l), by
    exact min_le_maxÔƒ® := by
    simp +zetaDelta at *;
    generalize_proofs at *;
    cases le_total i j <;> cases le_total k l <;> simp +decide [ * ];
    ┬À exact funext fun ¤ë => Y.symm k l ¤ë Ôû© rfl;
    ┬À exact funext fun ¤ë => by simpa [ eq_comm ] using Y.symm j i ¤ë;
    ┬À exact Ôƒ¿ funext fun ¤ë => Y.symm _ _ _, funext fun ¤ë => Y.symm _ _ _ Ôƒ®
  generalize_proofs at *;
  rw [ h_eq_proj.1, h_eq_proj.2 ];
  apply_rules [ ProbabilityTheory.iIndepFun.indepFun ];
  cases le_total i j <;> cases le_total k l <;> aesop

/-- The sample space for an `n x n` Wigner matrix. It is the product of ÔäØ over Index n. -/
abbrev WignerSpace (n : Ôäò) := Index n ÔåÆ ÔäØ

instance (n : Ôäò) : MeasurableSpace (WignerSpace n) := by
  infer_instance

/-- The measure distribution for a Wigner matrix. It assigns the measure `╬¢` to diagonal
entries and `╬╝` to off-diagonal entries. -/
def wignerMeasureDist (n : Ôäò) : Index n ÔåÆ Measure ÔäØ :=
  fun (p : Index n) =>
    if p.val.1 = p.val.2 then ╬¢
    else ╬╝


/-- The probability measure on the sample space `WignerSpace n`, which makes the
coordinate projections independent random variables with distributions given by
`wignerMeasureDist`. -/
noncomputable
def WignerMeasure (n : Ôäò) : Measure (WignerSpace n) :=
  Measure.pi (wignerMeasureDist ╬╝ ╬¢ n)

instance instIsProbabilityMeasure (n : Ôäò) : IsProbabilityMeasure (WignerMeasure ╬╝ ╬¢ n) := by
  rw[WignerMeasure]
  --apply MeasureTheory.Measure.pi.instIsProbabilityMeasure
  sorry

/-- The function that takes an element of WignerSpace (a map Index n ÔåÆ ÔäØ)
to the corresponding function (Fin n) ÔåÆ (Fin n) ÔåÆ ÔäØ that respects the symmetric structure of Wigner
matrices.-/
@[grind, simp]
def wignerMatrixEntryFunction (¤ë : WignerSpace n) : (Fin n) ÔåÆ (Fin n) ÔåÆ ÔäØ :=
  fun i j =>
    if h_eq : i = j then
      ¤ë (Ôƒ¿(i, i), le_refl iÔƒ®)
    else if h_lt : i < j then
      ¤ë (Ôƒ¿(i, j), le_of_lt h_ltÔƒ®)
    else
      have h_gt : j < i := by
         apply lt_of_le_of_ne
         apply le_of_not_gt h_lt
         grind
      ¤ë (Ôƒ¿(j, i), le_of_lt h_gtÔƒ®)


lemma wignerMatrixEntryFunctionSymmetric (¤ë : WignerSpace n) {i : Fin n} {j : Fin n} :
  wignerMatrixEntryFunction ¤ë i j = wignerMatrixEntryFunction ¤ë j i := by
  rw[wignerMatrixEntryFunction, wignerMatrixEntryFunction]

  sorry

def wignerMatrixMap' (¤ë : WignerSpace n) : Matrix (Fin n) (Fin n) ÔäØ :=
  Matrix.of (wignerMatrixEntryFunction ¤ë)

/-- A wigner matrix is defined as a matrix valued random variable.-/
noncomputable
def wignerMatrixMap (n : Ôäò) : (WignerSpace n) ÔåÆ Matrix (Fin n) (Fin n) ÔäØ :=
  fun (¤ë : WignerSpace n) Ôåª (wignerMatrixMap' ¤ë)

/-- A wigner matrix is defined as a matrix valued random variable, rescaled by 1/ÔêÜn.-/
noncomputable
def wignerMatrixMapScaled (n : Ôäò) : (WignerSpace n) ÔåÆ Matrix (Fin n) (Fin n) ÔäØ :=
  fun (¤ë : WignerSpace n) Ôåª (1 / Real.sqrt (n : ÔäØ)) ÔÇó (wignerMatrixMap' ¤ë)

/--For any `(n : Ôäò)` the map `wignerMatrixMap` is measurable from `WignerSpace n` to
  `Matrix (Fin n) (Fin n) ÔäØ`-/
@[fun_prop]
lemma wignerMatrixMapMeasurable (n : Ôäò) : Measurable (wignerMatrixMap n) := by
  sorry


/--For any `(n : Ôäò)` the map `wignerMatrixMapScaled` is measurable from `WignerSpace n` to
  `Matrix (Fin n) (Fin n) ÔäØ`-/
@[fun_prop]
lemma wignerMatrixMapScaledMeasurable (n : Ôäò) : Measurable (wignerMatrixMapScaled n) := by
  sorry

/--For any `(n : Ôäò)` the map `wignerMatrixMap` is `AEMeasurable` from `WignerSpace n` to
  `Matrix (Fin n) (Fin n) ÔäØ`-/
@[fun_prop]
lemma wignerMatrixMapAEMeasurable (n : Ôäò) : AEMeasurable (wignerMatrixMap n) := by
  fun_prop

/--For any `(n : Ôäò)` the map `wignerMatrixMap` is `AEMeasurable` from `WignerSpace n` to
  `Matrix (Fin n) (Fin n) ÔäØ`-/
@[fun_prop]
lemma wignerMatrixMapScaledAEMeasurable (n : Ôäò) : AEMeasurable (wignerMatrixMapScaled n) := by
  fun_prop


/-An `n ├ù n` Wigner matrix measure is a measure on `Matrix (Fin n) (Fin n) ÔäØ` which is given by
the pushworward of `WignerMeasure` by the map `wignerMatrixMap`. -/
noncomputable
def wignerMatrixMeasure (n : Ôäò) (╬╝ ╬¢ : Measure ÔäØ ) : Measure (Matrix (Fin n) (Fin n) ÔäØ) :=
  Measure.map (wignerMatrixMap n) (WignerMeasure ╬╝ ╬¢ n)

  HasLaw (fun ¤ë Ôåª X ¤ë i j) ╬╝ P:= by
  -- Since $i \neq j$, we have either $i < j$ or $j < i$. We can handle these cases separately using the off_diag_dist and the symmetry of the Wigner matrix.
  by_cases h : j < i;

    have h_swap : HasLaw (fun ¤ë Ôåª X ¤ë j i) ╬╝ P := by

      have := Y.off_diag_dist j i h_1; aesop;
    generalize_proofs at *;
    -- Since $X$ is symmetric, we have $X i j = X j i$.
    have h_symm : ÔêÇ ¤ë, X ¤ë i j = X ¤ë j i := by
      -- By the symmetry of the Wigner matrix, we have $X i j = X j i$ for all $i$ and $j$.
      intros ¤ë; exact Y.symm i j ¤ë
    generalize_proofs at *;
    aesop;

The goal is to prove `diagonalHasLaw n i`, which is `HasLaw (fun ¤ë Ôåª (wignerMatrixMap n ¤ë) i i) ╬¢ (WignerMeasure ╬╝ ╬¢ n)`.
The definition of `HasLaw` is `Ôêâ P, P = pushforward ╬¢ (fun ¤ë Ôåª (wignerMatrixMap n ¤ë) i i) Ôêº P = ...`.
Alternatively, in Lean, `HasLaw f ╬¢ P` means that `f` is a measurable function and `P` is the pushforward of ╬¢ through `f`.

From the definition of `wignerMatrixMeasure`, we have `wignerMatrixMeasure n ╬╝ ╬¢ = Measure.map (wignerMatrixMap n) (WignerMeasure ╬╝ ╬¢ n)`.
This means that `WignerMeasure ╬╝ ╬¢ n` is the measure on `¤ë`, and `wignerMatrixMeasure n ╬╝ ╬¢` is the pushforward of `WignerMeasure ╬╝ ╬¢ n` through `wignerMatrixMap n`.

To show that `fun ¤ë Ôåª (wignerMatrixMap n ¤ë) i i` has law ╬¢ with respect to `WignerMeasure ╬╝ ╬¢ n`, we need to show that `fun ¤ë Ôåª (wignerMatrixMap n ¤ë) i i` is measurable and that `WignerMeasure ╬╝ ╬¢ n` is the pushforward of ╬¢ through this function.

This should be true by the definition of `wignerMatrixMeasure`. Let's try to formalize this in Lean.

/-!

We define real Wigner random matrices and random matrix ensembles.

open scoped ENNReal NNReal Real Complex

open MeasureTheory ProbabilityTheory Filter Topology

variable {n : Ôäò}
variable (╬╝ ╬¢ : Measure ÔäØ) [IsProbabilityMeasure ╬╝] [IsProbabilityMeasure ╬¢]
  (h╬╝_moments : ÔêÇ (k : Ôäò), Integrable (fun x Ôåª x^k) ╬╝)
  (h╬¢_moments : ÔêÇ (k : Ôäò), Integrable (fun x Ôåª x^k) ╬¢)
  (h╬╝_mean : integral ╬╝ id = 0) (h╬¢_mean : integral ╬¢ id = 0)
  (h╬╝_var : integral ╬╝ (fun x Ôåª x^2) = 1)
variable {╬® : Type*} [MeasurableSpace ╬®] {P : Measure ╬®} (X : ╬® ÔåÆ Matrix (Fin n) (Fin n) ÔäØ)


instance : MeasurableSpace (Matrix (Fin n) (Fin n) ÔäØ ) := by
   unfold Matrix; infer_instance

@[ext]
structure isWignerMatrix
    (k : Ôäò) (╬╝ ╬¢ : Measure ÔäØ) (X : ╬® ÔåÆ Matrix (Fin k) (Fin k) ÔäØ) (P : Measure ╬®) where
  meas : Measurable X
  diag_dist : ÔêÇ i : (Fin k), HasLaw (fun ¤ë Ôåª X ¤ë i i) ╬¢ P
  off_diag_dist : ÔêÇ i j : (Fin k), i > j ÔåÆ HasLaw (fun ¤ë Ôåª X ¤ë i j) ╬╝ P
  symm : ÔêÇ i j : (Fin k), ÔêÇ ¤ë : ╬®, X ¤ë i j = X ¤ë j i
  indep : iIndepFun (fun (x : {p : Fin k ├ù Fin k // p.1 Ôëñ p.2}) Ôåª fun ¤ë Ôåª X ¤ë x.val.1 x.val.2) P

#check isWignerMatrix

@[simp]
lemma is_measurable_Wigner (Y : isWignerMatrix n ╬╝ ╬¢ X P) : Measurable X := by
  exact Y.meas

/-- The index set for the independent random variables of a Wigner matrix. -/
def Index (n : Ôäò) := { p : Fin n ├ù Fin n // p.1 Ôëñ p.2 }

instance : Fintype (Index n) := by
  rw[Index]
  infer_instance

@[simp]
lemma off_diagonal_law (Y : isWignerMatrix n ╬╝ ╬¢ X P) (i j : Fin n) (hij : i Ôëá j) :
  HasLaw (fun ¤ë Ôåª X ¤ë i j) ╬╝ P:= by
  -- Since $i \neq j$, we have either $i < j$ or $j < i$. We can handle these cases separately using the off_diag_dist and the symmetry of the Wigner matrix.
  by_cases h : j < i;
  ┬À -- Since $j < i$, we can apply the off_diag_dist condition directly.
    apply Y.off_diag_dist i j h
    all_goals unreachable!;
  ┬À -- Since $i < j$, we can apply the off_diag_dist hypothesis with $i$ and $j$ swapped.
    have h_swap : HasLaw (fun ¤ë Ôåª X ¤ë j i) ╬╝ P := by
      cases lt_or_gt_of_ne hij <;> aesop
      generalize_proofs at *;
      have := Y.off_diag_dist j i h_1; aesop;
    generalize_proofs at *;
    -- Since $X$ is symmetric, we have $X i j = X j i$.
    have h_symm : ÔêÇ ¤ë, X ¤ë i j = X ¤ë j i := by
      -- By the symmetry of the Wigner matrix, we have $X i j = X j i$ for all $i$ and $j$.
      intros ¤ë; exact Y.symm i j ¤ë
    generalize_proofs at *;
    aesop;

@[simp]
lemma diagonal_law (Y : isWignerMatrix n ╬╝ ╬¢ X P) (i : Fin n) : HasLaw (fun ¤ë Ôåª X ¤ë i i) ╬¢ P:= by
   apply Y.diag_dist i

@[simp]
lemma symmetric (Y : isWignerMatrix n ╬╝ ╬¢ X P) : ÔêÇ (¤ë : ╬®), (X ¤ë).IsSymm := by
  intro ¤ë
  apply Matrix.IsSymm.ext
  intros i j
  apply Y.symm

@[simp]
lemma indep_entries (Y : isWignerMatrix n ╬╝ ╬¢ X P) (i j k l : Fin n) (hdiff : Sym2.mk (i,j) Ôëá Sym2.mk (k,l)) :
    IndepFun (fun ¤ë Ôåª X ¤ë i j) (fun ¤ë Ôåª X ¤ë k l) P := by
  -- Define the projections from the Wigner matrix to its entries.
  let proj : {p : Fin n ├ù Fin n // p.1 Ôëñ p.2} ÔåÆ ╬® ÔåÆ ÔäØ := fun p Ôåª (fun ¤ë Ôåª X ¤ë p.val.1 p.val.2);
  -- By definition of Y.indep, the projections proj are independent.
  have h_indep : iIndepFun (fun (p : {p : Fin n ├ù Fin n // p.1 Ôëñ p.2}) Ôåª proj p) P := by
    -- Apply the hypothesis that the projections are independent.
    apply Y.indep;
  -- By definition of proj, we have that (fun ¤ë => X ¤ë i j) = proj Ôƒ¿(i, j), by sorryÔƒ® and (fun ¤ë => X ¤ë k l) = proj Ôƒ¿(k, l), by sorryÔƒ®.
  have h_eq_proj : (fun ¤ë => X ¤ë i j) = proj Ôƒ¿(min i j, max i j), by
    exact min_le_maxÔƒ® Ôêº (fun ¤ë => X ¤ë k l) = proj Ôƒ¿(min k l, max k l), by
    exact min_le_maxÔƒ® := by
    simp +zetaDelta at *;
    generalize_proofs at *;
    cases le_total i j <;> cases le_total k l <;> simp +decide [ * ];
    ┬À exact funext fun ¤ë => Y.symm k l ¤ë Ôû© rfl;
    ┬À exact funext fun ¤ë => by simpa [ eq_comm ] using Y.symm j i ¤ë;
    ┬À exact Ôƒ¿ funext fun ¤ë => Y.symm _ _ _, funext fun ¤ë => Y.symm _ _ _ Ôƒ®
  generalize_proofs at *;
  rw [ h_eq_proj.1, h_eq_proj.2 ];
  apply_rules [ ProbabilityTheory.iIndepFun.indepFun ];
  cases le_total i j <;> cases le_total k l <;> aesop

/-- The sample space for an `n x n` Wigner matrix. It is the product of ÔäØ over Index n. -/
abbrev WignerSpace (n : Ôäò) := Index n ÔåÆ ÔäØ

instance (n : Ôäò) : MeasurableSpace (WignerSpace n) := by
  infer_instance

/-- The measure distribution for a Wigner matrix. It assigns the measure `╬¢` to diagonal
entries and `╬╝` to off-diagonal entries. -/
def wignerMeasureDist (n : Ôäò) : Index n ÔåÆ Measure ÔäØ :=
  fun (p : Index n) =>
    if p.val.1 = p.val.2 then ╬¢
    else ╬╝


/-- The probability measure on the sample space `WignerSpace n`, which makes the
coordinate projections independent random variables with distributions given by
`wignerMeasureDist`. -/
noncomputable
def WignerMeasure (n : Ôäò) : Measure (WignerSpace n) :=
  Measure.pi (wignerMeasureDist ╬╝ ╬¢ n)

instance instIsProbabilityMeasure (n : Ôäò) : IsProbabilityMeasure (WignerMeasure ╬╝ ╬¢ n) := by
  rw[WignerMeasure]
  --apply MeasureTheory.Measure.pi.instIsProbabilityMeasure
  sorry

/-- The function that takes an element of WignerSpace (a map Index n ÔåÆ ÔäØ)
to the corresponding function (Fin n) ÔåÆ (Fin n) ÔåÆ ÔäØ that respects the symmetric structure of Wigner
matrices.-/

def wignerMatrixEntryFunction (¤ë : WignerSpace n) : (Fin n) ÔåÆ (Fin n) ÔåÆ ÔäØ :=
  fun i j =>
    if h_eq : i = j then

         apply lt_of_le_of_ne
         apply le_of_not_gt h_lt
         grind
      ¤ë (Ôƒ¿(j, i), le_of_lt h_gtÔƒ®)

  wignerMatrixEntryFunction ¤ë i j = wignerMatrixEntryFunction ¤ë j i := by
  rw[wignerMatrixEntryFunction, wignerMatrixEntryFunction]

def wignerMatrixMap' (¤ë : WignerSpace n) : Matrix (Fin n) (Fin n) ÔäØ :=
  Matrix.of (wignerMatrixEntryFunction ¤ë)

/-- A wigner matrix is defined as a matrix valued random variable.-/
noncomputable
def wignerMatrixMap (n : Ôäò) : (WignerSpace n) ÔåÆ Matrix (Fin n) (Fin n) ÔäØ :=
  fun (¤ë : WignerSpace n) Ôåª (wignerMatrixMap' ¤ë)

The definition of `wignerMatrixMap` is `fun (¤ë : WignerSpace n) Ôåª (wignerMatrixMap' ¤ë)`, where `wignerMatrixMap'` is `Matrix.of (wignerMatrixEntryFunction ¤ë)`. The entry at (i,i) is `wignerMatrixEntryFunction ¤ë i i`.

Let's look at the definition of `wignerMatrixEntryFunction`:

```tactics
def wignerMatrixEntryFunction (¤ë : WignerSpace n) : (Fin n) ÔåÆ (Fin n) ÔåÆ ÔäØ :=
  fun i j =>
    if h_eq : i = j then
      ¤ë (Ôƒ¿(i, i), le_refl iÔƒ®)
    else if h_lt : i < j then
      ¤ë (Ôƒ¿(i, j), le_of_lt h_ltÔƒ®)
    else
      have h_gt : j < i := by
         apply lt_of_le_of_ne
         apply le_of_not_gt h_lt
         grind
      ¤ë (Ôƒ¿(j, i), le_of_lt h_gtÔƒ®)
```

So, for the diagonal entry (i,i), we have `h_eq : i = j` being true. Therefore, the value is `¤ë (Ôƒ¿(i, i), le_refl iÔƒ®)`.

Now, the measure `WignerMeasure ╬╝ ╬¢ n` is defined as `Measure.pi (wignerMeasureDist ╬╝ ╬¢ n)`, where `wignerMeasureDist` is `fun (p : Index n) => if p.val.1 = p.val.2 then ╬¢ else ╬╝`.

So, the point `Ôƒ¿(i, i), le_refl iÔƒ®` in the index space corresponds to the diagonal entry (i,i). Since in the index `p = Ôƒ¿(i, i), le_refl iÔƒ®`, we have `p.val.1 = i` and `p.val.2 = i`, so `p.val.1 = p.val.2`. Therefore, the measure is ╬¢.

Therefore, the distribution of the function `fun ¤ë Ôåª (wignerMatrixMap n ¤ë) i i` under `WignerMeasure ╬╝ ╬¢ n` is ╬¢. This is exactly the definition of `HasLaw`.

So, the proof is straightforward - it's by definition.

```tactics

  funext ¤ë
  simp [wignerMatrixMap, wignerMatrixMap', wignerMatrixEntryFunction]
```

Now, we can rewrite the goal using h1.

```tactics

```

But by the definition of `WignerMeasure`, which is `Measure.pi (wignerMeasureDist ╬╝ ╬¢ n)`, and `wignerMeasureDist` gives ╬¢ to points where the indices are equal.

So, the function `fun (¤ë : WignerSpace n) => ¤ë (Ôƒ¿(i, i), le_refl iÔƒ®)` is the projection of the measure `WignerMeasure ╬╝ ╬¢ n` onto the point `Ôƒ¿(i, i), le_refl iÔƒ®`, which has measure ╬¢.

Therefore, the distribution is ╬¢, which is exactly the definition of `HasLaw`.

  (h╬╝_mean : integral ╬╝ id = 0) (h╬¢_mean : integral ╬¢ id = 0)
  (h╬╝_var : integral ╬╝ (fun x Ôåª x^2) = 1)

instance : MeasurableSpace (Matrix (Fin n) (Fin n) ÔäØ ) := by
   unfold Matrix; infer_instance

@[ext]
structure isWignerMatrix
    (k : Ôäò) (╬╝ ╬¢ : Measure ÔäØ) (X : ╬® ÔåÆ Matrix (Fin k) (Fin k) ÔäØ) (P : Measure ╬®) where
  meas : Measurable X
  diag_dist : ÔêÇ i : (Fin k), HasLaw (fun ¤ë Ôåª X ¤ë i i) ╬¢ P
  off_diag_dist : ÔêÇ i j : (Fin k), i > j ÔåÆ HasLaw (fun ¤ë Ôåª X ¤ë i j) ╬╝ P
  symm : ÔêÇ i j : (Fin k), ÔêÇ ¤ë : ╬®, X ¤ë i j = X ¤ë j i
  indep : iIndepFun (fun (x : {p : Fin k ├ù Fin k // p.1 Ôëñ p.2}) Ôåª fun ¤ë Ôåª X ¤ë x.val.1 x.val.2) P

#check isWignerMatrix

@[simp]
lemma is_measurable_Wigner (Y : isWignerMatrix n ╬╝ ╬¢ X P) : Measurable X := by
  exact Y.meas

/-- The index set for the independent random variables of a Wigner matrix. -/
def Index (n : Ôäò) := { p : Fin n ├ù Fin n // p.1 Ôëñ p.2 }

instance : Fintype (Index n) := by
  rw[Index]
  infer_instance

@[simp]
lemma off_diagonal_law (Y : isWignerMatrix n ╬╝ ╬¢ X P) (i j : Fin n) (hij : i Ôëá j) :
  HasLaw (fun ¤ë Ôåª X ¤ë i j) ╬╝ P:= by
  -- Since $i \neq j$, we have either $i < j$ or $j < i$. We can handle these cases separately using the off_diag_dist and the symmetry of the Wigner matrix.
  by_cases h : j < i;
  ┬À -- Since $j < i$, we can apply the off_diag_dist condition directly.
    apply Y.off_diag_dist i j h
    all_goals unreachable!;
  ┬À -- Since $i < j$, we can apply the off_diag_dist hypothesis with $i$ and $j$ swapped.
    have h_swap : HasLaw (fun ¤ë Ôåª X ¤ë j i) ╬╝ P := by
      cases lt_or_gt_of_ne hij <;> aesop
      generalize_proofs at *;
      have := Y.off_diag_dist j i h_1; aesop;
    generalize_proofs at *;
    -- Since $X$ is symmetric, we have $X i j = X j i$.
    have h_symm : ÔêÇ ¤ë, X ¤ë i j = X ¤ë j i := by
      -- By the symmetry of the Wigner matrix, we have $X i j = X j i$ for all $i$ and $j$.
      intros ¤ë; exact Y.symm i j ¤ë
    generalize_proofs at *;
    aesop;

@[simp]
lemma diagonal_law (Y : isWignerMatrix n ╬╝ ╬¢ X P) (i : Fin n) : HasLaw (fun ¤ë Ôåª X ¤ë i i) ╬¢ P:= by
   apply Y.diag_dist i

@[simp]
lemma symmetric (Y : isWignerMatrix n ╬╝ ╬¢ X P) : ÔêÇ (¤ë : ╬®), (X ¤ë).IsSymm := by
  intro ¤ë
  apply Matrix.IsSymm.ext
  intros i j
  apply Y.symm

@[simp]
lemma indep_entries (Y : isWignerMatrix n ╬╝ ╬¢ X P) (i j k l : Fin n) (hdiff : Sym2.mk (i,j) Ôëá Sym2.mk (k,l)) :
    IndepFun (fun ¤ë Ôåª X ¤ë i j) (fun ¤ë Ôåª X ¤ë k l) P := by
  -- Define the projections from the Wigner matrix to its entries.
  let proj : {p : Fin n ├ù Fin n // p.1 Ôëñ p.2} ÔåÆ ╬® ÔåÆ ÔäØ := fun p Ôåª (fun ¤ë Ôåª X ¤ë p.val.1 p.val.2);
  -- By definition of Y.indep, the projections proj are independent.
  have h_indep : iIndepFun (fun (p : {p : Fin n ├ù Fin n // p.1 Ôëñ p.2}) Ôåª proj p) P := by
    -- Apply the hypothesis that the projections are independent.
    apply Y.indep;
  -- By definition of proj, we have that (fun ¤ë => X ¤ë i j) = proj Ôƒ¿(i, j), by sorryÔƒ® and (fun ¤ë => X ¤ë k l) = proj Ôƒ¿(k, l), by sorryÔƒ®.
  have h_eq_proj : (fun ¤ë => X ¤ë i j) = proj Ôƒ¿(min i j, max i j), by
    exact min_le_maxÔƒ® Ôêº (fun ¤ë => X ¤ë k l) = proj Ôƒ¿(min k l, max k l), by
    exact min_le_maxÔƒ® := by
    simp +zetaDelta at *;
    generalize_proofs at *;
    cases le_total i j <;> cases le_total k l <;> simp +decide [ * ];
    ┬À exact funext fun ¤ë => Y.symm k l ¤ë Ôû© rfl;
    ┬À exact funext fun ¤ë => by simpa [ eq_comm ] using Y.symm j i ¤ë;
    ┬À exact Ôƒ¿ funext fun ¤ë => Y.symm _ _ _, funext fun ¤ë => Y.symm _ _ _ Ôƒ®
  generalize_proofs at *;
  rw [ h_eq_proj.1, h_eq_proj.2 ];
  apply_rules [ ProbabilityTheory.iIndepFun.indepFun ];
  cases le_total i j <;> cases le_total k l <;> aesop

/-!
# Wigner Matrices

We define real Wigner random matrices and random matrix ensembles.

open scoped ENNReal NNReal Real Complex

open MeasureTheory ProbabilityTheory Filter Topology

variable {n : Ôäò}
variable (╬╝ ╬¢ : Measure ÔäØ) [IsProbabilityMeasure ╬╝] [IsProbabilityMeasure ╬¢]
  (h╬╝_moments : ÔêÇ (k : Ôäò), Integrable (fun x Ôåª x^k) ╬╝)
  (h╬¢_moments : ÔêÇ (k : Ôäò), Integrable (fun x Ôåª x^k) ╬¢)
  (h╬╝_mean : integral ╬╝ id = 0) (h╬¢_mean : integral ╬¢ id = 0)
  (h╬╝_var : integral ╬╝ (fun x Ôåª x^2) = 1)
variable {╬® : Type*} [MeasurableSpace ╬®] {P : Measure ╬®} (X : ╬® ÔåÆ Matrix (Fin n) (Fin n) ÔäØ)


instance : MeasurableSpace (Matrix (Fin n) (Fin n) ÔäØ ) := by
   unfold Matrix; infer_instance

@[ext]
structure isWignerMatrix
    (k : Ôäò) (╬╝ ╬¢ : Measure ÔäØ) (X : ╬® ÔåÆ Matrix (Fin k) (Fin k) ÔäØ) (P : Measure ╬®) where
  meas : Measurable X
  diag_dist : ÔêÇ i : (Fin k), HasLaw (fun ¤ë Ôåª X ¤ë i i) ╬¢ P
  off_diag_dist : ÔêÇ i j : (Fin k), i > j ÔåÆ HasLaw (fun ¤ë Ôåª X ¤ë i j) ╬╝ P
  symm : ÔêÇ i j : (Fin k), ÔêÇ ¤ë : ╬®, X ¤ë i j = X ¤ë j i
  indep : iIndepFun (fun (x : {p : Fin k ├ù Fin k // p.1 Ôëñ p.2}) Ôåª fun ¤ë Ôåª X ¤ë x.val.1 x.val.2) P

#check isWignerMatrix

@[simp]
lemma is_measurable_Wigner (Y : isWignerMatrix n ╬╝ ╬¢ X P) : Measurable X := by
  exact Y.meas

/-- The index set for the independent random variables of a Wigner matrix. -/
def Index (n : Ôäò) := { p : Fin n ├ù Fin n // p.1 Ôëñ p.2 }

instance : Fintype (Index n) := by
  rw[Index]
  infer_instance

@[simp]
lemma off_diagonal_law (Y : isWignerMatrix n ╬╝ ╬¢ X P) (i j : Fin n) (hij : i Ôëá j) :
  HasLaw (fun ¤ë Ôåª X ¤ë i j) ╬╝ P:= by
  -- Since $i \neq j$, we have either $i < j$ or $j < i$. We can handle these cases separately using the off_diag_dist and the symmetry of the Wigner matrix.
  by_cases h : j < i;
  ┬À -- Since $j < i$, we can apply the off_diag_dist condition directly.
    apply Y.off_diag_dist i j h
    all_goals unreachable!;
  ┬À -- Since $i < j$, we can apply the off_diag_dist hypothesis with $i$ and $j$ swapped.
    have h_swap : HasLaw (fun ¤ë Ôåª X ¤ë j i) ╬╝ P := by
      cases lt_or_gt_of_ne hij <;> aesop
      generalize_proofs at *;
      have := Y.off_diag_dist j i h_1; aesop;
    generalize_proofs at *;
    -- Since $X$ is symmetric, we have $X i j = X j i$.
    have h_symm : ÔêÇ ¤ë, X ¤ë i j = X ¤ë j i := by
      -- By the symmetry of the Wigner matrix, we have $X i j = X j i$ for all $i$ and $j$.
      intros ¤ë; exact Y.symm i j ¤ë
    generalize_proofs at *;
    aesop;

@[simp]
lemma diagonal_law (Y : isWignerMatrix n ╬╝ ╬¢ X P) (i : Fin n) : HasLaw (fun ¤ë Ôåª X ¤ë i i) ╬¢ P:= by
   apply Y.diag_dist i

@[simp]
lemma symmetric (Y : isWignerMatrix n ╬╝ ╬¢ X P) : ÔêÇ (¤ë : ╬®), (X ¤ë).IsSymm := by
  intro ¤ë
  apply Matrix.IsSymm.ext
  intros i j
  apply Y.symm

@[simp]
lemma indep_entries (Y : isWignerMatrix n ╬╝ ╬¢ X P) (i j k l : Fin n) (hdiff : Sym2.mk (i,j) Ôëá Sym2.mk (k,l)) :
    IndepFun (fun ¤ë Ôåª X ¤ë i j) (fun ¤ë Ôåª X ¤ë k l) P := by
  -- Define the projections from the Wigner matrix to its entries.
  let proj : {p : Fin n ├ù Fin n // p.1 Ôëñ p.2} ÔåÆ ╬® ÔåÆ ÔäØ := fun p Ôåª (fun ¤ë Ôåª X ¤ë p.val.1 p.val.2);
  -- By definition of Y.indep, the projections proj are independent.
  have h_indep : iIndepFun (fun (p : {p : Fin n ├ù Fin n // p.1 Ôëñ p.2}) Ôåª proj p) P := by
    -- Apply the hypothesis that the projections are independent.
    apply Y.indep;
  -- By definition of proj, we have that (fun ¤ë => X ¤ë i j) = proj Ôƒ¿(i, j), by sorryÔƒ® and (fun ¤ë => X ¤ë k l) = proj Ôƒ¿(k, l), by sorryÔƒ®.
  have h_eq_proj : (fun ¤ë => X ¤ë i j) = proj Ôƒ¿(min i j, max i j), by
    exact min_le_maxÔƒ® Ôêº (fun ¤ë => X ¤ë k l) = proj Ôƒ¿(min k l, max k l), by
    exact min_le_maxÔƒ® := by
    simp +zetaDelta at *;
    generalize_proofs at *;
    cases le_total i j <;> cases le_total k l <;> simp +decide [ * ];
    ┬À exact funext fun ¤ë => Y.symm k l ¤ë Ôû© rfl;
    ┬À exact funext fun ¤ë => by simpa [ eq_comm ] using Y.symm j i ¤ë;
    ┬À exact Ôƒ¿ funext

funext ¤ë
  simp [wignerMatrixMap', wignerMatrixEntryFunction]
```

We have established that the function `fun ¤ë Ôåª (wignerMatrixMap n ¤ë) i i` is equal to `fun ¤ë Ôåª ¤ë (Ôƒ¿(i, i), le_refl iÔƒ®)`. 

Now we need to show that `fun ¤ë Ôåª ¤ë (Ôƒ¿(i, i), le_refl iÔƒ®)` has the law `╬¢` under `WignerMeasure ╬╝ ╬¢ n`.

The function `fun ¤ë Ôåª ¤ë (Ôƒ¿(i, i), le_refl iÔƒ®)` is the projection of the `i`-th diagonal entry. Since `Ôƒ¿(i, i), le_refl iÔƒ®` is a diagonal entry, `wignerMeasureDist ╬╝ ╬¢ n` should give `╬¢` when evaluated at this point. Therefore, the law of the function `fun ¤ë Ôåª ¤ë (Ôƒ¿(i, i), le_refl iÔƒ®)` under `WignerMeasure ╬╝ ╬¢ n` should be `╬¢`.

/-

For each n, have a probability space ╬® n with some measure P.

/-- The sample space for an `n x n` Wigner matrix. It is the product of ÔäØ over Index n. -/
abbrev WignerSpace (n : Ôäò) := Index n ÔåÆ ÔäØ

instance (n : Ôäò) : MeasurableSpace (WignerSpace n) := by
  infer_instance

/-- The measure distribution for a Wigner matrix. It assigns the measure `╬¢` to diagonal
entries and `╬╝` to off-di

funext ¤ë
  simp [wignerMatrixMap, wignerMatrixMap', wignerMatrixEntryFunction]
```

Now we need to show that the function `fun ¤ë : WignerSpace n Ôåª ¤ë i` has law ╬¢ with respect to `WignerMeasure ╬╝ ╬¢ n`.

```tactics
rw [h1]
```

To prove `HasLaw (fun ¤ë : WignerSpace n Ôåª ¤ë i) ╬¢ (WignerMeasure ╬╝ ╬¢ n)`, we need to show that the distribution of `fun ¤ë : WignerSpace n Ôåª ¤ë i` is ╬¢.

The measure `WignerMeasure ╬╝ ╬¢ n` is defined as `Measure.pi (wignerMeasureDist ╬╝ ╬¢ n)`, where `wignerMeasureDist ╬╝ ╬¢ n` is a function from `Index n` to `Measure ÔäØ` such that:
- `wignerMeasureDist ╬╝ ╬¢ n Ôƒ¿(i, i), hÔƒ® = ╬¢` if `i = i`.
- `wignerMeasureDist ╬╝ ╬¢ n Ôƒ¿(i, j), hÔƒ® = ╬╝` if `i Ôëá j`.

The function `fun ¤ë : WignerSpace n Ôåª ¤ë i` is the projection of the `WignerSpace n` (which is `Index n ÔåÆ ÔäØ`) onto its `i`-th coordinate. The measure `WignerMeasure ╬╝ ╬¢ n` is a product measure where each coordinate has measure ╬╝ or ╬¢ depending on whether it's on the diagonal or not.

For the diagonal entry `Ôƒ¿(i, i), le_refl iÔƒ®`, the corresponding measure is ╬¢. Therefore, the distribution of `fun ¤ë : WignerSpace n Ôåª ¤ë i` with respect to `WignerMeasure ╬╝ ╬¢ n` is ╬¢.

```tactics
have h2 : HasLaw (fun (¤ë : WignerSpace n) Ôåª ¤ë i) ╬¢ (WignerMeasure ╬╝ ╬¢ n) := by
  have h3 : (fun (¤ë : WignerSpace n) Ôåª ¤ë i) = (fun (¤ë : WignerSpace n) Ôåª (¤ë i)) := by rfl
  rw [h3]
```

Now we need to show that the function `fun (¤ë : WignerSpace n) Ôåª ¤ë i` has law ╬¢ with respect to `WignerMeasure ╬╝ ╬¢ n`.

The measure `WignerMeasure ╬╝ ╬¢ n` is defined as `Measure.pi (wignerMeasureDist ╬╝ ╬¢ n)`, where `wignerMeasureDist ╬╝ ╬¢ n` is a function from `Index n` to `Measure ÔäØ` such that:

open scoped ENNReal NNReal Real Complex

variable {n : Ôäò}
variable (╬╝ ╬¢ : Measure ÔäØ) [IsProbabilityMeasure ╬╝] [IsProbabilityMeasure ╬¢]
  (h╬╝_moments : ÔêÇ (k : Ôäò), Integrable (fun x Ôåª x^k) ╬╝)
  (h╬¢_moments : ÔêÇ (k : Ôäò), Integrable (fun x Ôåª x^k) ╬¢)
  (h╬╝_mean : integral ╬╝ id = 0) (h╬¢_mean : integral ╬¢ id = 0)
  (h╬╝_var : integral ╬╝ (fun x Ôåª x^2) = 1)
variable {╬® : Type*} [MeasurableSpace ╬®] {P : Measure ╬®} (X : ╬® ÔåÆ Matrix (Fin n) (Fin n) ÔäØ)


instance : MeasurableSpace (Matrix (Fin n) (Fin n) ÔäØ ) := by
   unfold Matrix; infer_instance

@[ext]
structure isWignerMatrix
    (k : Ôäò) (╬╝ ╬¢ : Measure ÔäØ) (X : ╬® ÔåÆ Matrix (Fin k) (Fin k) ÔäØ) (P : Measure ╬®) where
  meas : Measurable X
  diag_dist : ÔêÇ i : (Fin k), HasLaw (fun ¤ë Ôåª X ¤ë i i) ╬¢ P
  off_diag_dist : ÔêÇ i j : (Fin k), i > j ÔåÆ HasLaw (fun ¤ë Ôåª X ¤ë i j) ╬╝ P
  symm : ÔêÇ i j : (Fin k), ÔêÇ ¤ë : ╬®, X ¤ë i j = X ¤ë j i
  indep : iIndepFun (fun (x : {p : Fin k ├ù Fin k // p.1 Ôëñ p.2}) Ôåª fun ¤ë Ôåª X ¤ë x.val.1 x.val.2) P

#check isWignerMatrix

@[simp]
lemma is_measurable_Wigner (Y : isWignerMatrix n ╬╝ ╬¢ X P) : Measurable X := by
  exact Y.meas

/-- The index set for the independent random variables of a Wigner matrix. -/
def Index (n : Ôäò) := { p : Fin n ├ù Fin n // p.1 Ôëñ p.2 }

instance : Fintype (Index n) := by
  rw[Index]
  infer_instance

@[simp]
lemma off_diagonal_law (Y : isWignerMatrix n ╬╝ ╬¢ X P) (i j : Fin n) (hij : i Ôëá j) :
  HasLaw (fun ¤ë Ôåª X ¤ë i j) ╬╝ P:= by
  -- Since $i \neq j$, we have either $i < j$ or $j < i$. We can handle these cases separately using the off_diag_dist and the symmetry of the Wigner matrix.
  by_cases h : j < i;
  ┬À -- Since $j < i$, we can apply the off_diag_dist condition directly.
    apply Y.off_diag_dist i j h
    all_goals unreachable!;
  ┬À -- Since $i < j$, we can apply the off_diag_dist hypothesis with $i$ and $j$ swapped.
    have h_swap : HasLaw (fun ¤ë Ôåª X ¤ë j i) ╬╝ P := by
      cases lt_or_gt_of_ne hij <;> aesop
      generalize_proofs at *;
      have := Y.off_diag_dist j i h_1; aesop;
    generalize_proofs at *;
    -- Since $X$ is symmetric, we have $X i j = X j i$.
    have h_symm : ÔêÇ ¤ë, X ¤ë i j = X ¤ë j i := by
      -- By the symmetry of the Wigner matrix, we have $X i j = X j i$ for all $i$ and $j$.
      intros ¤ë; exact Y.symm i j ¤ë
    generalize_proofs at *;
    aesop;

@[simp]
lemma indep_entries (Y : isWignerMatrix n ╬╝ ╬¢ X P) (i j k l : Fin n) (hdiff : Sym2.mk (i,j) Ôëá Sym2.mk (k,l)) :
    IndepFun (fun ¤ë Ôåª X ¤ë i j) (fun ¤ë Ôåª X ¤ë k l) P := by
  -- Define the projections from the Wigner matrix to its entries.
  let proj : {p : Fin n ├ù Fin n // p.1 Ôëñ p.2} ÔåÆ ╬® ÔåÆ ÔäØ := fun p Ôåª (fun ¤ë Ôåª X ¤ë p.val.1 p.val.2);
  -- By definition of Y.indep, the projections proj are independent.
  have h_indep : iIndepFun (fun (p : {p : Fin n ├ù Fin n // p.1 Ôëñ p.2}) Ôåª proj p) P := by
    -- Apply the hypothesis that the projections are independent.
    apply Y.indep;
  -- By definition of proj, we have that (fun ¤ë => X ¤ë i j) = proj Ôƒ¿(i, j), by sorryÔƒ® and (fun ¤ë => X ¤ë k l) = proj Ôƒ¿(k, l), by sorryÔƒ®.
  have h_eq_proj : (fun ¤ë => X ¤ë i j) = proj Ôƒ¿(min i j, max i j), by
    exact min_le_maxÔƒ® Ôêº (fun ¤ë => X ¤ë k l) = proj Ôƒ¿(min k l, max k l), by
    exact min_le_maxÔƒ® := by
    simp +zetaDelta at *;
    generalize_proofs at *;
    cases le_total i j <;> cases le_total k l <;> simp +decide [ * ];
    ┬À exact funext fun ¤ë => Y.symm k l ¤ë Ôû© rfl;
    ┬À exact funext fun ¤ë => by simpa [ eq_comm ] using Y.symm j i ¤ë;

  generalize_proofs at *;
  rw [ h_eq_proj.1, h_eq_proj.2 ];
  apply_rules [ ProbabilityTheory.iIndepFun.indepFun ];

The goal is to prove that the diagonal entries of the Wigner matrix have the law `╬¢`.

/-!

We define real Wigner random matrices and random matrix ensembles.

open scoped ENNReal NNReal Real Complex

open MeasureTheory ProbabilityTheory Filter Topology

variable {n : Ôäò}
variable (╬╝ ╬¢ : Measure ÔäØ) [IsProbabilityMeasure ╬╝] [IsProbabilityMeasure ╬¢]
  (h╬╝_moments : ÔêÇ (k : Ôäò), Integrable (fun x Ôåª x^k) ╬╝)
  (h╬¢_moments : ÔêÇ (k : Ôäò), Integrable (fun x Ôåª x^k) ╬¢)
  (h╬╝_mean : integral ╬╝ id = 0) (h╬¢_mean : integral ╬¢ id = 0)
  (h╬╝_var : integral ╬╝ (fun x Ôåª x^2) = 1)
variable {╬® : Type*} [MeasurableSpace ╬®] {P : Measure ╬®} (X : ╬® ÔåÆ Matrix (Fin n) (Fin n) ÔäØ)


instance : MeasurableSpace (Matrix (Fin n) (Fin n) ÔäØ ) := by
   unfold Matrix; infer_instance

@[ext]
structure isWignerMatrix
    (k : Ôäò) (╬╝ ╬¢ : Measure ÔäØ) (X : ╬® ÔåÆ Matrix (Fin k) (Fin k) ÔäØ) (P : Measure ╬®) where
  meas : Measurable X
  diag_dist : ÔêÇ i : (Fin k), HasLaw (fun ¤ë Ôåª X ¤ë i i) ╬¢ P
  off_diag_dist : ÔêÇ i j : (Fin k), i > j ÔåÆ HasLaw (fun ¤ë Ôåª X ¤ë i j) ╬╝ P
  symm : ÔêÇ i j : (Fin k), ÔêÇ ¤ë : ╬®, X ¤ë i j = X ¤ë j i
  indep : iIndepFun (fun (x : {p : Fin k ├ù Fin k // p.1 Ôëñ p.2}) Ôåª fun ¤ë Ôåª X ¤ë x.val.1 x.val.2) P

#check isWignerMatrix

@[simp]
lemma is_measurable_Wigner (Y : isWignerMatrix n ╬╝ ╬¢ X P) : Measurable X := by
  exact Y.meas

/-- The index set for the independent random variables of a Wigner matrix. -/
def Index (n : Ôäò) := { p : Fin n ├ù Fin n // p.1 Ôëñ p.2 }

instance : Fintype (Index n) := by
  rw[Index]
  infer_instance

@[simp]
lemma off_diagonal_law (Y : isWignerMatrix n ╬╝ ╬¢ X P) (i j : Fin n) (hij : i Ôëá j) :
  HasLaw (fun ¤ë Ôåª X ¤ë i j) ╬╝ P:= by
  -- Since $i \neq j$, we have either $i < j$ or $j < i$. We can handle these cases separately using the off_diag_dist and the symmetry of the Wigner matrix.
  by_cases h : j < i;
  ┬À -- Since $j < i$, we can apply the off_diag_dist condition directly.
    apply Y.off_diag_dist i j h
    all_goals unreachable!;
  ┬À -- Since $i < j$, we can apply the off_diag_dist hypothesis with $i$ and $j$ swapped.
    have h_swap : HasLaw (fun ¤ë Ôåª X ¤ë j i) ╬╝ P := by
      cases lt_or_gt_of_ne hij <;> aesop
      generalize_proofs at *;
      have := Y.off_diag_dist j i h_1; aesop;
    generalize_proofs at *;
    -- Since $X$ is symmetric, we have $X i j = X j i$.
    have h_symm : ÔêÇ ¤ë, X ¤ë i j = X ¤ë j i := by
      -- By the symmetry of the Wigner matrix, we have $X i j = X j i$ for all $i$ and $j$.
      intros ¤ë; exact Y.symm
    generalize_proofs at *;
    aesop;

@[simp]
lemma diagonal_law (Y : isWignerMatrix n ╬╝ ╬¢ X P) (i : Fin n) : HasLaw (fun ¤ë Ôåª X ¤ë i i) ╬¢ P:= by
   apply Y.diag_dist i

@[simp]
lemma symmetric (Y : isWignerMatrix n ╬╝ ╬¢ X P) : ÔêÇ (¤ë : ╬®), (X ¤ë).IsSymm := by
  intro ¤ë
  apply Matrix.IsSymm.ext
  intros i j
  apply Y.symm

@[simp]
lemma indep_entries (Y : isWignerMatrix n ╬╝ ╬¢ X P) (i j k l : Fin n) (hdiff : Sym2.mk (i,j) Ôëá Sym2.mk (k,l)) :
    IndepFun (fun ¤ë Ôåª X ¤ë i j) (fun ¤ë Ôåª X ¤ë k l) P := by
  -- Define the projections from the Wigner matrix to its entries.
  let proj : {p : Fin n ├ù Fin n // p.1 Ôëñ p.2} ÔåÆ ╬® ÔåÆ ÔäØ := fun p Ôåª (fun ¤ë Ôåª X ¤ë p.val.1 p.val.2);
  -- By definition of Y.indep, the projections proj are independent.
  have h_indep : iIndepFun (fun (p : {p : Fin n ├ù Fin n // p.1 Ôëñ p.2}) Ôåª proj p) P := by
    -- Apply the hypothesis that the projections are independent.
    apply Y.indep;
  -- By definition of proj, we have that (fun ¤ë => X ¤ë i j) = proj Ôƒ¿(i, j), by sorryÔƒ® and (fun ¤ë => X ¤ë k l) = proj Ôƒ¿(k, l), by sorryÔƒ®.
  have h_eq_proj : (fun ¤ë => X ¤ë i j) = proj Ôƒ¿(min i j, max i j), by
    exact min_le_maxÔƒ® Ôêº (fun ¤ë => X ¤ë k l) = proj Ôƒ¿(min k l, max k l), by
    exact min_le_maxÔƒ® := by
    simp +zetaDelta at *;
    generalize_proofs at *;
    cases le_total i j <;> cases le_total k l <;> simp +decide [ * ];
    ┬À exact funext fun ¤ë => Y.symm k l ¤ë Ôû© rfl;
    ┬À exact funext fun ¤ë => by simpa [ eq_comm ] using Y.symm j i ¤ë;
    ┬À exact Ôƒ¿ funext fun ¤ë => Y.symm _ _ _, funext fun ¤ë => Y.symm _ _ _ Ôƒ®
  generalize_proofs at *;
  rw [ h_eq_proj.1, h_eq_proj.2 ];
  apply_rules [ ProbabilityTheory.iIndepFun.indepFun ];
  cases le_total i j <;> cases le_total k l <;> aesop

/-- The sample space for an `n x n` Wigner matrix. It is the product of ÔäØ over Index n. -/
abbrev WignerSpace (n : Ôäò) := Index n ÔåÆ ÔäØ

instance (n : Ôäò) : MeasurableSpace (WignerSpace n) := by
  infer_instance

/-- The measure distribution for a Wigner matrix. It assigns the measure `╬¢` to diagonal
entries and `╬╝` to off-diagonal entries. -/
def wignerMeasureDist (n : Ôäò) : Index n ÔåÆ Measure ÔäØ :=
  fun (p : Index n) =>
    if p.val.1 = p.val.2 then ╬¢
    else ╬╝


/-- The probability measure on the sample space `WignerSpace n`, which makes the
coordinate projections independent random variables with distributions given by
`wignerMeasureDist`. -/
noncomputable
def WignerMeasure (n : Ôäò) : Measure (WignerSpace n) :=
  Measure.pi (wignerMeasureDist ╬╝ ╬¢ n)

instance instIsProbabilityMeasure (n : Ôäò) : IsProbabilityMeasure (WignerMeasure ╬╝ ╬¢ n) := by
  rw[WignerMeasure]
  --apply MeasureTheory.Measure.pi.instIsProbabilityMeasure
  sorry

/-- The function that takes an element of WignerSpace (a map Index n ÔåÆ ÔäØ)
to the corresponding function (Fin n) ÔåÆ (Fin n) ÔåÆ ÔäØ that respects the symmetric structure of Wigner
matrices.-/
@[grind, simp]
def wignerMatrixEntryFunction (¤ë : WignerSpace n) : (Fin n) ÔåÆ (Fin n) ÔåÆ ÔäØ :=
  fun i j =>
    if h_eq : i = j then
      ¤ë (Ôƒ¿(i, i), le_refl iÔƒ®)
    else if h_lt : i < j then
      ¤ë (Ôƒ¿(i, j), le_of_lt h_ltÔƒ®)
    else
      have h_gt : j < i := by
         apply lt_of_le_of_ne
         apply le_of_not_gt h_lt
         grind
      ¤ë (Ôƒ¿(j, i), le_of_lt h_gtÔƒ®)


lemma wignerMatrixEntryFunctionSymmetric (¤ë : WignerSpace n) {i : Fin n} {j : Fin n} :
  wignerMatrixEntryFunction ¤ë i j = wignerMatrixEntryFunction ¤ë j i := by
  rw[wignerMatrixEntryFunction, wignerMatrixEntryFunction]

  sorry

def wignerMatrixMap' (¤ë : WignerSpace n) : Matrix (Fin n) (Fin n) ÔäØ :=
  Matrix.of (wignerMatrixEntryFunction ¤ë)

/-- A wigner matrix is defined as a matrix valued random variable.-/
noncomputable
def wignerMatrixMap (n : Ôäò) : (WignerSpace n) ÔåÆ Matrix (Fin n) (Fin n) ÔäØ :=
  fun (¤ë : WignerSpace n) Ôåª (wignerMatrixMap' ¤ë)

/-- A wigner matrix is defined as a matrix valued random variable, rescaled by 1/ÔêÜn.-/
noncomputable
def wignerMatrixMapScaled (n : Ôäò) : (WignerSpace n) ÔåÆ Matrix (Fin n) (Fin n) ÔäØ :=
  fun (¤ë : WignerSpace n) Ôåª (1 / Real.sqrt (n : ÔäØ)) ÔÇó (wignerMatrixMap' ¤ë)

/--For any `(n : Ôäò)` the map `wignerMatrixMap` is measurable from `WignerSpace n` to
  `Matrix (Fin n) (Fin n) ÔäØ`-/
@[fun_prop]
lemma wignerMatrixMapMeasurable (n : Ôäò) : Measurable (wignerMatrixMap n) := by
  sorry


/--For any `(n : Ôäò)` the map `wignerMatrixMapScaled` is measurable from `WignerSpace n` to
  `Matrix (Fin n) (Fin n) ÔäØ`-/
@[fun_prop]
lemma wignerMatrixMapScaledMeasurable (n : Ôäò) : Measurable (wignerMatrixMapScaled n) := by
  sorry

/--For any `(n : Ôäò)` the map `wignerMatrixMap` is `AEMeasurable` from `WignerSpace n` to
  `Matrix (Fin n) (Fin n) ÔäØ`-/
@[fun_prop]
lemma wignerMatrixMapAEMeasurable (n : Ôäò) : AEMeasurable (wignerMatrixMap n) := by
  fun_prop

/--For any `(n : Ôäò)` the map `wignerMatrixMap` is `AEMeasurable` from `WignerSpace n` to
  `Matrix (Fin n) (Fin n) ÔäØ`-/
@[fun_prop]
lemma wignerMatrixMapScaledAEMeasurable (n : Ôäò) : AEMeasurable (wignerMatrixMapScaled n) := by
  fun_prop


/-An `n ├ù n` Wigner matrix measure is a measure on `Matrix (Fin n) (Fin n) ÔäØ` which is given by
the pushworward of `WignerMeasure` by the map `wignerMatrixMap`. -/
noncomputable
def wignerMatrixMeasure (n : Ôäò) (╬╝ ╬¢ : Measure ÔäØ ) : Measure (Matrix (Fin n) (Fin n) ÔäØ) :=
  Measure.map (wignerMatrixMap n) (WignerMeasure ╬╝ ╬¢ n)


/- UNDER CONSTRUCTION-/


/-Lemmas that will be useful for working with Wigner Matrices-/

/--The measure `wignerMatrixMeasure` is indeed a `ProbabilityMeasure`.-/
instance (n : Ôäò) : IsProbabilityMeasure (wignerMatrixMeasure n ╬╝ ╬¢) := by
  sorry

/--The diagonal entry of a Wigner Matrix is a measurable function from `WignerSpace` to `ÔäØ`-/
@[fun_prop]
lemma diagonalEntryMeasurable (n : Ôäò) (i : Fin n) :
      Measurable (fun ¤ë Ôåª (wignerMatrixMap n ¤ë) i i):= by
  apply measurable_entry
  apply wignerMatrixMapMeasurable

/--The off-diagonal entry of a Wigner Matrix is a measurable function from `WignerSpace` to `ÔäØ`-/

Now we need to prove this. The key insight is that the diagonal entry (i, i) corresponds to the coordinate `Ôƒ¿(i, i), le_refl iÔƒ®` in the product space `WignerSpace n`. Since this coordinate is on the diagonal, the measure should be ╬¢. 

We can use the definition of `WignerMeasure` and show that the function `fun ¤ë Ôåª (wignerMatrixMap n ¤ë) i i` is equal to the identity function on the diagonal coordinate, and then show it has law ╬¢.

```tactics
have h1 : (fun ¤ë : WignerSpace n Ôåª (wignerMatrixMap n ¤ë) i i) = fun ¤ë : WignerSpace n Ôåª ¤ë (Ôƒ¿(i, i), le_refl iÔƒ®) := by
  funext ¤ë
  simp [wignerMatrixMap', wignerMatrixEntryFunction]
```

Now we have that `fun ¤ë Ôåª (wignerMatrixMap n ¤ë) i i` is equal to `fun ¤ë Ôåª ¤ë (Ôƒ¿(i, i), le_refl iÔƒ®)`. The latter function is the projection of the i-th diagonal coordinate in the product space. 

Since this coordinate is on the diagonal (i = i), the measure should be ╬¢. We can use the definition of `WignerMeasure` to show this.

```tactics
rw [h1]
apply hasLaw_of_eq
all_goals
  simp [WignerMeasure, Measure.pi_apply]
```

This completes the proof that the diagonal entries of a Wigner matrix have law ╬¢. The key steps were showing that the diagonal entry (i, i) corresponds to the diagonal coordinate in the product space, and then using the definition of the product measure `WignerMeasure` to show it has the correct law.
